Title: How to Act Like a Spoiled Girl Introduction: This guide is intended for entertainment purposes only and should not be used to manipulate or deceive others. It's important to remember that being spoiled isn't an attractive quality and can lead to negative consequences such as damaged relationships and a lack of gratitude. That said, if you want to act like a spoiled girl for Halloween, a play, or just for fun, here are some steps to help you get into character. Step 1: Develop a Sense of Entitlement To act like a spoiled girl, you need to feel entitled to special treatment at all times. This means believing that you deserve things simply because you want them, regardless of whether you have earned them or not. To develop this mindset, practice thinking about what you want and expecting to receive it without question. For example, if you see a toy you like in a store, tell yourself that you must have it, even if your parents initially say no. Key tip: Remember, acting this way in real life will likely make people dislike you, so try to limit this behavior to situations where it won't hurt anyone's feelings. Step 2: Adopt a Demanding Attitude Once you believe you deserve special treatment, start demanding it from those around you. Use phrases like "I want" instead of "please." Insist on getting your own way by throwing tantrums when things don't go your way. Make sure to whine and complain loudly until someone gives in to your demands. Guideline: Be careful not to overdo it, as constant complaining can become annoying quickly. Step 3: Show Disregard for Others' Feelings Spoiled girls often prioritize their wants and needs above everyone else's. Practice ignoring other people's opinions and desires by focusing solely on what you want. If someone disagrees with you, dismiss their concerns as unimportant. However, keep in mind that treating people poorly may damage your relationships, so consider saving this behavior for performance scenarios. Step 4: Display Excessive Pride in Your Belongings A spoiled girl takes great pride in her possessions, especially expensive ones. When showing off your belongings, do so with exaggerated confidence and boastfulness. Talk about how much they cost and emphasize how lucky you are to have them. Keep in mind that flaunting wealth can come across as arrogant, so again, save this behavior for appropriate contexts. Step 5: Expect Perfection From Those Around You As a spoiled girl, you expect everything to be done exactly the way you want it. Train yourself to nitpick every detail and criticize others openly when they fail to meet your expectations. While doing so, maintain a haughty attitude that suggests superiority over others. Note: Being hypercritical may create tension among friends and family members, so avoid adopting this trait outside of roleplaying situations. Step 6: Learn to Manipulate People Through Pouting and Crying When faced with resistance, learn to pout or cry dramatically to manipulate others into giving in to your demands. Study videos of children having meltdowns in public places for inspiration. Just remember that using tears to control people in real life is manipulative and unfair. Conclusion: While learning to act like a spoiled girl can be entertaining for short-term roleplaying scenarios, it's essential to recognize that behaving this way consistently has severe downsides. Instead of trying to embody these traits permanently, consider practicing them temporarily for costume parties, theater productions, or similar events. And always treat others with kindness and respect! Title: How to Brush a Doll's Hair Like a Pro Introduction: Doll hair can become tangled or matted over time, making your beloved doll look unkempt. Regular brushing is essential to maintain its appearance and prevent damage to the hair. This comprehensive guide will provide you with an in-depth explanation of how to brush a doll's hair properly, ensuring beautiful locks that stay tangle-free. Materials Needed: 1. A soft-bristle brush (preferably one designed specifically for dolls) 2. A wide-tooth comb 3. Gentle shampoo and conditioner (if necessary) 4. Clean water 5. A towel 6. Patience and care! Step 1: Gather Your Materials Before starting, make sure you have all required materials at hand. Having everything ready ensures smooth progress without any interruptions during the process. Step 2: Prepare the Doll's Hair If the doll's hair hasn't been brushed in a while, start by gently detangling small sections using your fingers. Be patient; this may take some time but reduces potential breakage caused by forceful brushing later on. Key Tip: Never pull or yank on knots as it could cause irreversible harm to the hair. Instead, try loosening them gradually with gentle finger action. Step 3: Apply Conditioner (Optional) For severely tangled or dirty hair, dampen the hair slightly with clean water and apply a pea-sized amount of mild conditioner evenly throughout the strands. Avoid soaking the hair because excessive moisture weakens synthetic fibers leading to easy breakage. Let it sit for about five minutes before rinsing thoroughly under running lukewarm water. Remember to squeeze out excess water gently after washing. Step 4: Divide the Hair into Sections Divide the doll's hair into manageable sections - usually four quadrants work well. Secure these divisions with clips or rubber bands to keep them separate and organized while working through each section individually. Step 5: Start Brushing from the Ends Beginning at the ends instead of the roots allows you to remove smaller snarls more easily without pushing bigger ones towards the scalp where they become harder to tackle. Use light strokes moving upwards slowly until there are no visible tangles left. Step 6: Switch to Combing Once most of the major tangles have been removed with the brush, switch to a wide-tooth comb. Starting again from the bottom, carefully draw the comb upward, smoothing out any remaining minor knots along the way. The wider space between teeth minimizes pulling and stress on the hair. Step 7: Smooth Down the Roots Finally, go back to the brush and lightly stroke downward near the roots to eliminate parting lines and add shine. Always be cautious around the crown and delicate areas like bangs or ponytails. Step 8: Style As Desired After successfully brushing and detangling, feel free to restyle your doll's hair according to preference. Whether you opt for braids, curls, or simply leaving it loose, ensure it looks neat and tidy. Conclusion: Proper maintenance of a doll's hair involves regular brushing to avoid buildup of dirt and tangles. By following this thorough guide, you now possess the knowledge needed to keep your doll looking fabulous with minimal risk of damaging its lovely locks. Happy styling! Tunicates are sessile, but their larvae are mobile, and have some features found in early vertebrates. The process of paedomorphosis, where juvenile features are retained in the adult, is the proposed mechanism. Genome analysis does show that the tunicates are the closest living relatives of vertebrates. Once there was a thin girl named Claire. Claire always wanted to feel relaxed, but couldn't find a way. She was too weighed down by her worries. She wanted to feel as light as a feather, so she could float away. One day she decided to go find a way to relax. She ran and ran until she stumbled upon a beautiful pond. It sparkled in the sun, like a thousand diamonds. She stepped in and felt the cool water wrap around her feet. Slowly, she relaxed. She walked deeper into the middle of the pond, feeling the weight from her worries slowly wash away. Claire let out a deep breath and smiled as she felt the water kiss her skin. The gentle sway of the water seemed to help her mind feel better as she floated on top. Her worries seemed weightless, and she felt free and happy for the first time in a long time. Claire stayed there for a while and enjoyed the peace, until she was ready to go home. She emerged from the pond feeling thin and relaxed. She could never forget the magic of the pond, and still visits it whenever she needs to feel the weightless freedom it brings. That's what we did here what we did here. I'll give it to Sis if she can have. The Solar System - Grades: 1-2, 3-5 Earth is one of nine known planets that go around our star, the sun. Other objects go around the sun, too - large chunks of rocks called asteroids, balls of ice called comets, and dust particles. All of these belong to our solar system. Our solar system is in the Milky Way galaxy. There are millions of other systems in this galaxy. The Milky Way galaxy is part of the universe. From Earth, the other planets look flat. But they are really shaped more like balls. Planets move in two ways. Every planet turns around and around like a spinning top. This movement is called rotating. As each planet rotates, it also follows a path around the sun. The path is called an orbit. We say the planet orbits (revolves around) the sun. Something else makes our solar system work the way it does. It is a force called gravity. Gravity - the Greatest Force Of All When you hold a pencil in your hand and let it go, it falls to the ground. Why doesn't it float in the air? A great scientist, Albert Einstein, explained why. Everything is made up of matter. The amount of matter an object has is called its mass. Some objects have great mass. Some have little. In space objects with great mass pull other objects to them. That pull is a force called gravity. Because Earth's mass is great, its pull of gravity - or gravitational [GRAV-it-TAY-shun-ul] force - is also great. It pulls the pencil toward Earth's center and keeps it from floating in air. Gravity keeps us from floating in air, too. The sun, which is a million times larger than Earth, contains an enormous amount of matter. Its mass is far greater than Earth's. The sun's gravitational force is very strong. If it were not, a planet would move in a straight line out into space forever. The sun's gravity pulls the planet toward the sun, which changes the straight line of direction into a curve. This keeps the planet moving in an orbit around the sun. Because of the sun's gravitational pull, all the planets in our solar system orbit around it. The sun is a huge ball of super-hot gas. Deep inside the sun, the temperature is hotter than the hottest furnace. The sun is so hot that it heats and lights all the planets in our solar system. The Orbits Of The Planets Some planets orbit closer to the sun than others. And some are far away from the sun. The planets that are farthest from the sun are called the outer planets. The planets that are closer to the sun are called the inner planets. The inner planets are close to each other as well as close to the sun. There are four inner planets: The inner planets have a hard, rocky surface. It is possible to land a spacecraft on planets that have a hard surface. The five outer planets are not only farther from the sun, but they are also far apart from one another. Four of them are much, much bigger than the inner planets. The four giant planets are: Scientists believe that the surface of these huge planets is not solid. It is probably slushy. The last planet discovered in our solar system is farthest away from the sun. It is Pluto. We don't know much about Pluto yet except that it is very, very cold. Pluto is not like the other outer planets. And it is not like the inner planets, either. Pluto is a mystery. Adapted from Scholastic's "A Book About Planets and Stars" by Betty Polisar Reigot. Title: How to Freeze Water: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Water freezing is a natural process that occurs when its temperature drops below 0 degrees Celsius (32 degrees Fahrenheit). This phenomenon has various applications in our daily lives, from preserving food to creating refreshing beverages during hot weather. By following this detailed guide, you will learn how to freeze water effectively while gaining insights into the science behind the process. Step 1: Choose the Right Container Select an appropriate container made of freezer-safe materials such as plastic, glass, or stainless steel. The shape and size of the container can affect the freezing rate; shallow and wide containers allow faster freezing than deep and narrow ones due to increased surface area exposure to cold air. Key tip: Avoid using metal containers because they may cause uneven cooling and lead to potential damage from rapid expansion during freezing. Step 2: Fill the Container Properly Pour clean water into the chosen container up to about three quarters full. Leaving some space at the top prevents excessive pressure buildup caused by ice expansion, reducing chances of container cracking or bursting. Guideline: If freezing large quantities of water, divide it among multiple smaller containers instead of filling one big container to ensure even freezing throughout. Step 3: Place Containers in the Freezer Position the filled containers carefully onto a level shelf within your freezer, ensuring there's enough room for proper air circulation around them. Restricting airflow might result in slower freezing times and inconsistent temperatures. Tip: To speed up the freezing process, set your freezer temperature to its lowest setting before placing the containers inside. Remember to reset it back to normal after the water has frozen. Step 4: Monitor the Freezing Progress Keep an eye on the progress of the freezing water every 15-20 minutes until initial signs of solidification appear. Ice formation typically starts along the edges and gradually moves towards the center. Observing this stage provides valuable insight into how quickly your freezer operates under specific conditions. Step 5: Allow Complete Freezing Once the outer layer of water turns into ice, let the rest continue freezing undisturbed until completely solidified. Depending on factors like initial water temperature, container material, and freezer efficiency, this could take anywhere between 2-4 hours for small volumes (e.g., 500ml) and longer for larger amounts. Patience is crucial here, as rushing the process might compromise the quality of the frozen water. Step 6: Store Frozen Water Correctly After achieving complete solidification, transfer the sealed containers to their designated storage spot within the freezer. Maintain consistent organization to facilitate easy retrieval later on. Always keep frozen water away from raw meats or pungent foods to prevent cross-contamination and unwanted flavors seeping into the ice. Step 7: Regularly Check and Rotate Stock Periodically inspect your frozen water supply to check for any signs of leakage, spoilage, or off-odors. Discard any suspicious items promptly. Additionally, practice regular rotation by moving older batches towards the front and newer ones towards the back. This habit ensures timely consumption of oldest stocks first and minimizes waste due to forgotten or expired items. Conclusion: By meticulously adhering to these steps and incorporating best practices, anyone can successfully freeze water with ease. Understanding the principles behind freezing allows individuals to maximize the benefits of this simple yet essential technique in both culinary and household contexts. Stay hydrated and enjoy your frosty creations! Or a bus and that's a taxi. I would like to try something now. I can see something I tell you later. Worded problems can be tricky, because they usually have both words and numbers in them, and figuring out how to work them out can be hard. We have started looking at 'HOW' to take out the important bits of information or 'data', so that we can make an equation and work out the problem. We have been using these steps to help us 1. Read the problem 2. Extract the data 3. Decide what type of calculation is needed (addition, subtraction, multiplication or division) 4. Show your working out 5. The original salami was made from a mix of chopped pork and salt which was dried using air in a casing. Jews & Muslims are not allowed to eat this type of salami for religious reasons. Now there are many types of salamis made in some countries. Nearly all are seasoned with a combination of herbs and spices in addition to salt. Salamis are now sometimes smoked or cooked before air drying. Some kinds are made of beef while others mix beef and pork. Most, if not all Italian salamis have garlic in them, but few German kinds do, for example. Some, like a few salamis from Spain, include paprika or chili. The difference between some types is in how coarse or fine the meat is chopped. Some "light" salami might add turkey or chicken to reduce both fat and calories. Title: How to Feed Butterflies Butterfly gardens are becoming increasingly popular as people look for ways to attract these beautiful creatures to their backyards. An essential part of maintaining a butterfly garden is providing an adequate food source for them. This tutorial will guide you through the process of feeding butterflies, ensuring they thrive in your garden. ### Step 1: Understand Butterfly Nutrition Before diving into the feeding methods, it's crucial to understand what butterflies eat. Adult butterflies primarily feed on nectar from flowers using their long proboscis, a tubular mouthpart that functions like a straw. However, some species also consume tree sap, fruit juices, or even water droplets. Caterpillars, on the other hand, have chewing mouthparts and eat leaves, stems, or other plant parts. Key Tip: Different butterfly species may have different dietary preferences, so offering various food options can help attract a wider variety of butterflies. ### Step 2: Provide Nectar-Rich Flowers The most natural way to feed adult butterflies is by growing plants with abundant nectar sources. Choose brightly colored flowers with flat tops or short tubes, which make it easier for butterflies to access the nectar. Some excellent choices include: * Asters * Black-eyed Susans * Butterfly bush * Daylilies * Lantanas * Marigolds * Milkweeds * Pentas * Purple coneflowers * Zinnias Key Guideline: Plant your nectar-rich flowers in groups to create large patches of color that are more visible and attractive to butterflies. Also, consider planting host plants specifically for caterpillars, such as milkweed for monarchs. ### Step 3: Create a Fruit Feeder Another option for feeding adult butterflies is by setting up a fruit feeder. To do this, follow these steps: 1. Cut ripe fruits (such as bananas, oranges, peaches, or watermelon) into slices or small chunks. 2. Place the fruit pieces on a shallow dish or tray, preferably one with raised edges to prevent the juice from spilling out. 3. Secure the dish to a post, fence, or tree trunk using wire, string, or another method that keeps it stable but still allows butterflies to perch while feeding. 4. Change the fruit every day or two to ensure freshness and prevent mold growth. Key Tip: Rotate the types of fruit offered to provide diverse nutrients and attract various butterfly species. ### Step 4: Set Up a Sugar Water Feeder You can also create a sugar water feeder for butterflies. Here's how: 1. Dissolve 1 part granulated white sugar in 4 parts warm water (e.g., cup sugar in 1 cup water). Stir until completely dissolved. June 9. [Nina is holding one of her teddy bears in front of the mirror] Creating a DIY (Do It Yourself) Pok dex can be an exciting and rewarding project, especially if you're a fan of the popular Pok mon franchise. A Pok dex is a device used by trainers in the game and anime series to catalog and provide information about various Pok mon species. This tutorial will guide you through creating your own functional Pok dex using readily available materials and tools. **Materials needed:** 1. A smartphone or tablet with internet access 2. Printer and paper 3. Cardstock or heavyweight printer paper 4. Clear adhesive sheets (optional) 5. Scissors or craft knife 6. Ruler 7. Glue stick or double-sided tape 8. Binder rings or large keyrings 9. Decorative elements such as stickers, markers, etc. (optional) **Step 1: Design your Pok dex layout** Before you start printing and cutting out pages, plan the layout of your Pok dex. You may want to include sections like a table of contents, individual Pok mon entries, regional Pok dexes, and other features from the games or animated series. Be sure to leave enough space within each entry to record relevant data such as type, height, weight, abilities, moveset, and evolution methods. **Key tip**: Keep in mind that some smartphones have larger screens than others; ensure your design allows easy reading on different devices. **Step 2: Create digital templates** Using word processing software or graphic design programs, create digital templates based on your chosen layout. Make sure they are formatted properly for both print and screen viewing. If possible, save them as PDF files so that their format remains consistent across multiple platforms. **Step 3: Gather Pok mon data** Research reliable sources online where you can find accurate and up-to-date information about all generations of Pok mon. Websites like Bulbapedia or Serebii are great resources for this purpose. Copy and paste necessary details into your template under corresponding headings. **Guideline**: Always verify information from at least two reputable websites before including it in your Pok dex. **Step 4: Print your pages** Print your completed templates onto regular printer paper first to check for any errors or issues. Once satisfied, reprint them onto cardstock or heavier weight paper for durability. Remember to set your printer settings to 'actual size' instead of 'fit to page'. **Step 5: Apply clear protective covers (Optional)** If desired, protect your printed pages with clear adhesive sheets. These can help prevent smudging, tearing, and make your Pok dex more resistant to wear and tear. Carefully cut around the edges of each sheet after application. **Step 6: Assemble the binder** Punch holes along one side of each printed page, ensuring alignment with the hole puncher. Attach these pages together using binder rings or large keyrings. Adjust the spacing between pages according to personal preference. **Step 7: Customize your Pok dex** Add finishing touches to your Pok dex by decorating its cover and spine with stickers, markers, or other embellishments. You could also add pockets inside the front and back covers to store notes, trading cards, or additional reference material. **Congratulations!** With these steps followed correctly, you now possess a fully functioning DIY Pok dex. Fill it with knowledge about every known Pok mon species, trade it with friends, and let it accompany you on countless adventures! [SIB is playing with the puzzle by herself] 100 Awesome Engineering Projects for Kids By Kristie Lewis Engineering and fun aren't always two things that kids naturally associate with one another, but there are hundreds of ways to make engineering, physics and design fun and challenging for kids. Here are 100 great experiments that will let kids construct, play, learn and grow, all while they study the fundamentals of engineering. These projects focus on the basics of motion, force and other essentials of physics. - Balls and Ramp. Try out this project to learn more about the laws of gravity. - Ping pong ball catapult. Let kids have fun flinging the harmless ping pong ball across the room with this great activity. - Topple dominoes. Make learning about physics fun with a few dominoes that you can set up and then knock down. - Build a confetti launcher. Make any day a party with this fun and engaging project. - Design a simple machine. Show how machines can make work easier with this flexible project. - Chocolate grabbing. This experiment explains concepts like the center of gravity to young learners. - Tower of coins. Learn about friction in this experiment using nickels. - Build a top. How long can you keep a top going? Find out in this experiment. - Moving cans. Getting two cans to move towards each other without touching them could be trickier than you think. - Create a pendulum. To better understand motion, consider this project that requires you to build a pendulum. - Paper clip trick. Children will be thrilled with this trick done with gravity. - Balloon Blast Off. With this project, kids will use string and a balloon to see if they can hit a target on the other side of the room. - Gravity basics. To understand why buildings don't topple over and how gravity really works, try out this project. - Pushover. This experiment will teach kids about Newton's 3rd Law of Motion. Light and Electricity Let kids have fun understanding how to harness light and electricity. - Create your own light bulb. Are your kids curious about how light bulbs are made? This project will let them make their own and see how the whole process works. - Pinhole camera. Photos can be a mysterious thing for kids who don't understand how they work. With a pinhole camera, they'll be able to see how the light is refracted onto film by creating a camera obscura. - Create a kaleidoscope. Kids will love seeing the colors and images created by the play of light in this experiment. - Build simple circuits. Everything from television to light switches run on circuits so let kids create their own with this project. - Split up light with a prism. This project will let children see just how light works and how we can manipulate it. - Make a lemon battery. Kids will be amazed at the energy drawn from this fruit. - Electrify gelatin. Raise up gelatin using only static electricity in this project. - Make an electroscope. Using this device, kids will be able to test for static electricity. - Steadiness tester. How steady are your hands? Find out in this experiment. - How light travels. If you want to harness light you have to know how it moves. Get a quick education from this set of experiments. Kids will have a blast trying to put together these building projects. - Build a Bird House. Kids can learn about building as well as nature with this idea. - Build your own bridge. Kids will learn how to engineer for strength in this classic building project. - Engineer your own high rise. Let kids reach for the sky in this building project. - Create a cardboard crane. You'll be able to see just what you can lift using this cardboard construction. - Design an earthquake-resistant structure. Help kids see how buildings can be made to flex and move with this simple project. - Build an egg tower. This project could be messy but it will give kids a chance to learn about how buildings are made strong. - Geodesic dome. There are special types of functions that have graph symmetry. The most notable types are even and odd functions. Even functions have graph symmetry across the y-axis, and if they are reflected, will give us the same function. Odd functions have 180 rotational graph symmetry, if they are rotated 180 about the origin we will get the same function. There are algebraic ways to compute if a function is even or odd. I want to talk about even and odd functions. First the definition. A function f is even if f of -x equals f of x for all x in the domain of f. That means that you can switch x for -x and get the same value. Now what kind of symmetry does that give us? Well the graph of an even function's always going to be symmetric with respect to y axis. Why is that? Well, if you remember our discussion of symmetry, of reflections, the graph of y equals f of -x. y equals f of -x is a reflection about the y axis, if the reflection of about the y axis of a function is exactly same the same as the function itself then it's symmetric about the y axis. Now let's look at two examples from our parent functions. There's y=x squared and there's y equals the absolute value of x. Now odd functions. Function f is odd if f of -x equals the opposite of f of x. This means that opposite inputs give opposite outputs. Now, if this is true, the graph of an odd function would be symmetrical with respect to the origin. That means is you could take the the graph, rotate it 180 degrees and it will look exactly the same. So it's 180 degrees symmetry about the origin. Now some examples from our parent functions are y=x, y equals x cubed and also y equals 1 over x. So remember odd functions: opposite inputs have opposite outputs. Even functions: opposite inputs have the same input. Even functions are symmetric about the y axis, odd functions are symmetric about the origin. The game is played in "innings". Professional baseball games have 9 innings. In an inning, each team has one turn to bat and try to score "runs", adding one point. When one team hits the ball, the other team defends and tries to get three players on the other team "out". The team that is playing defense always has the ball. This is different from other team sports. When the team on defense gets three players out, it is their turn to try and score runs. Then the team that was batting starts playing defense, and the team that was playing defense starts batting. After nine innings, the team that has the most runs is the winner. If the teams have the same number of runs, they play more innings until one team wins. At the start of the game, the home team pitches, while players on the visiting team bat. Only one player can bat at a time. What was Cory helping you to do? If you have two you can stay under two hours. oh, you don't even know your name? The Welsh alphabet has some extra letters that are not used in English, and does not have some others. Although certain letters do not exist in Welsh, they are used sometimes to make sounds that could not possibly be made otherwise. A good example is the word "garej" (meaning "garage"). The letter "j" does not exist in the Welsh language, and is a lend-word from English. The traditional word for "garage" in Welsh is "modurdy", which means, "motor house". Another lend-word is ""toiled"," which means "toilet" in English. There are now many lend-words in spoken Welsh. The title page of a book has the information about when and where the book was published. It will give you the name of the author. It will tell you the copyright date or the date the book was written or published. The table of contents in a book will help you to find the names of the chapters of the book and the page number where each chapter begins. Each chapter of the book will have a name and usually has a chapter number. The glossary of a book will give meanings to words used in the book. Vocabulary words or new words in the book will be here. The glossary can help you to understand your reading and will make it easier for you to learn and study the information. The index of a book is very valuable to learners. You will find a listing of each topic in the book, names of people, maps in the book, or any other information in the book listed here. This listing will be in alphabetical order and will have the page number in the book where you can find the information you are looking for. This is very helpful to learners. Take a few minutes to review a book and see if you can find all the parts of a book. Locating information can be easy and fun. ANSWER THE FOLLOWING: 1. They can range from 0.000000000001, which are barely heard, to 1 W/m (painfully loud). The decibel scale makes sound intensity numbers easier to work with. A 0.000000000001 W/m intensity is 0 dB (decibels). It is an exponential scale, so when the decibel number increases by 10, the intensity is ten times as much. So, a 1 W/m intensity is 120 dB. Title: How to Cook Perfect Popcorn in the Microwave Introduction: Microwaved popcorn is a convenient and delicious snack that can be enjoyed at home or office. This tutorial will guide you through the process of cooking perfect popcorn in the microwave with clear, detailed steps and helpful tips. Key Tips and Guidelines: 1. Always use a microwave-safe bowl or bag designed specifically for popping corn. 2. Never leave the microwave unattended while popping corn. 3. Listen carefully to identify when popping slows down significantly; this indicates readiness. 4. Allow steam to escape before consuming to avoid burning your mouth. 5. Customize your popcorn by adding seasonings after popping. Step 1: Choose Your Kernel Type There are two main types of popcorn kernels: white and yellow. White tends to have a slightly sweet flavor, while yellow has a richer, more traditional taste. Both work well for microwaving. Step 2: Measure Out the Kernels A general guideline is to use 1/4 cup (4 tablespoons) of kernels per 2.5 quarts of popped corn. Adjust based on personal preference and microwave wattage. Too many kernels may cause some to burn before others finish popping. Step 3: Prepare the Bowl or Bag If using a bowl, lightly coat its interior with vegetable oil spray or line it with parchment paper to prevent sticking. For bags, simply place them in the microwave as they come. Step 4: Add Oil and Seasonings (Optional) Drizzle about one teaspoon of olive or coconut oil over the kernels if desired. Sprinkle salt or other preferred seasonings like garlic powder, chili flakes, or nutritional yeast onto the oiled kernels. Toss gently to distribute evenly. Note: Some prefer air-popped popcorn without added fat. Step 5: Place the Bowl or Bag in the Microwave Ensure there's enough room inside the oven for the contents to expand fully. If possible, position the container towards the center of the turntable. Step 6: Set the Correct Time The ideal time varies depending on the microwave's power level. Start with 2 minutes on high heat then adjust according to results. Keep an eye on the progress during initial trials. Most batches take between 2-4 minutes. Step 7: Monitor Carefully During Cooking Listen closely once popping begins. When the rate of popping slows dramatically, usually around 1-2 seconds between pops, remove the bowl or bag immediately. Overcooked popcorn can become bitter and unpleasant. Step 8: Let It Rest Allow the freshly cooked popcorn to sit undisturbed for a minute so that excess steam can escape. This reduces moisture content, improving texture and preventing soggy popcorn. Step 9: Serve and Enjoy! Transfer the popcorn into serving bowls, removing any remaining unpopped kernels ("old maids"). Savor your homemade treat, knowing exactly what went into making it! Remember, practice makes perfect. Feel free to experiment with different kernel varieties, cook times, and seasonings until you find your favorite combination. Happy popping! Title: How to Delete a Playlist from an iPod Nano Deleting playlists from your iPod Nano can help you declutter your device or remove outdated collections. This tutorial will guide you through the process step-by-step, ensuring that even first-time users can confidently delete their unwanted playlists. **Key Tips & Guidelines:** 1. Make sure your iPod Nano is charged before starting this process to avoid any interruptions due to low battery. 2. Sync your iPod with iTunes after deleting a playlist if you want the changes to appear on your computer as well. 3. Be aware that deleting a playlist does not delete the songs within; they will remain in your library unless manually deleted. 4. The steps below are applicable for 7th generation iPod Nanos and later versions. If you have an older model, some interface elements might look different but the overall flow should be similar. **Step 1: Navigate to Your Playlists** * Press the center button (Select) on your iPod Nano to wake up the screen and go to the main menu. * Scroll down using the Click Wheel until you reach "Music." Press Select again. * Now scroll right using the Click Wheel until you see "Playlists". Press Select. This step takes you directly to where all your created playlists reside, making it easier for you to find the one(s) you wish to delete. **Step 2: Find the Playlist You Want to Delete** Once inside the "Playlists" section, browse through them by rotating the Click Wheel left or right. When you've found the playlist you want to delete, stop scrolling. **Step 3: Enter Edit Mode** After locating the target playlist, press and hold the Center button (Select). Keep holding it until the word "Edit" appears at the top right corner of the display. Release the button once "Edit" shows up. Entering edit mode allows us to make modifications such as renaming or deleting playlists. **Step 4: Initiate Deletion Process** With "Edit" now displayed at the top right corner, locate the 'Delete' option which should appear next to your selected playlist. It may show as a trash bin icon or simply say 'Delete'. Rotate the Click Wheel so that 'Delete' is highlighted then click the Center Button (Select). A confirmation popup message will appear asking if you really want to delete the playlist. Remember, this action cannot be undone without restoring your entire iPod via iTunes. **Step 5: Confirm Deletion** To confirm the deletion, navigate to 'Yes' using the Click Wheel and select it by pressing the Center Button (Select). Wait for a moment while your iPod removes the playlist. Once completed, you'll return to the previous screen showing the remaining playlists. If you accidentally chose 'No', just repeat Step 4 and choose 'Yes' when prompted again. **Step 6: Exit Edit Mode** Finally, exit edit mode by scrolling to the 'Done' option usually located at the bottom left of the screen. Highlight it and hit the Center Button (Select). This ensures no accidental edits occur moving forward. Congratulations! You've successfully deleted a playlist from your iPod Nano. Repeat these steps for additional playlists as needed. Don't forget to sync with iTunes afterward if necessary. I thought it was a choo choo train. Title: How to Fence in Close Quarters Introduction: Fencing in close quarters can be a challenging yet rewarding experience. It requires precision, agility, and quick decision-making skills. This guide will provide you with an in-depth understanding of fencing techniques that are effective in tight spaces. Whether you're new to fencing or looking to improve your skillset, this article has got you covered! Step 1: Understand Your Surroundings Before engaging in any combat, take a moment to analyze your environment. Look for potential obstacles such as walls, corners, or furniture that could limit your movement. Being aware of these factors will help you strategize better and avoid accidents during the match. Key Tip: Always keep a safe distance from any objects that might cause injury if contacted at high speed. Step 2: Adopt the Proper Stance Maintaining a stable and balanced stance is crucial when fencing in confined areas. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and weight evenly distributed between both legs. Hold your weapon firmly but relaxed, ready to strike at any given moment. Guideline: The ideal posture should allow easy mobility while providing stability and control over your movements. Step 3: Master Basic Footwork Footwork forms the foundation of all fencing techniques. Practice basic steps like lunge, retreat, advance, and lateral displacement. These moves enable you to maintain optimal distance from your opponent (called "measure") and create opportunities for attacks. Key Tip: To ensure efficiency, always move using the whole foot instead of just the toes or heels. Step 4: Utilize Compact Actions In close quarters, large swings and wide stances are impractical due to space constraints. Instead, focus on compact actions - short, precise strikes aimed at vulnerable spots. Use small steps and angled motions to evade counterattacks. Guideline: Remember, accuracy surpasses power in tight spaces; hence train yourself to aim correctly rather than forcefully. Step 5: Control Distance and Angles Managing distance ("measure") and angles becomes more critical in limited spaces. Try to position yourself diagonally relative to your adversary, which provides additional protection and creates offensive possibilities. Additionally, maintain a comfortable attacking range where you can land hits without being too close to invite counters. Key Tip: Regularly adjust your position based on your opponent's movements to prevent them from gaining advantageous positions. Step 6: Anticipate Opponent's Moves Predicting what your rival plans next is essential for success in fencing, especially in closed environments. Observe their body language, prior attack patterns, and reaction times to anticipate their future actions. Guideline: Never underestimate your opponents; they may have surprise elements up their sleeves. Stay alert and adaptable throughout the bout. Step 7: Train Consistently Like every other sport, mastery comes through consistent practice. Set aside time regularly to hone your skills, focusing particularly on close-quarter tactics. Seek feedback from experienced coaches or peers to identify areas needing improvement. Key Tip: Consider joining sparring sessions with diverse fencers to gain varied experiences and broaden your tactical knowledge. Conclusion: Remember, fencing in close quarters demands patience, discipline, and technical finesse. By following the outlined steps meticulously and incorporating the provided suggestions, you'll significantly enhance your ability to excel in constricted spaces. Happy fencing! Title: What is a Convex Hull and How Can We Use it? Have you ever played with shapes and tried to figure out the smallest fence or wall that could enclose them? That's similar to what we do when working with something called a "convex hull." Let me try my best to explain this concept without using any big words! Imagine you have a bunch of dots scattered on a piece of paper (or even better, draw some yourself!). Now your task is to find the smallest possible shape that surrounds all those dots without having any corners inside it. This shape would be like a rubber band stretched around all the dots so that it touches as many dots as possible while still keeping them contained. Most likely, this shape turns out to be a polygon - a fancy name for a shape with straight lines for sides, like triangles, squares, pentagons, etc. In real life, imagine throwing stones into a lake and tracing the outline formed by the splashes; that boundary line would represent the convex hull of the stone impacts. Or think about building a fence around a group of trees in your backyard - the fence would follow the contour of the convex hull surrounding the trees. Now why is this useful? Well, once we know the convex hull, we can answer lots of questions more easily. For instance, if we wanted to find the two points farthest away from one another within our dot collection, these extreme points are guaranteed to lie either on the same side or opposite sides of the convex hull. So instead of checking every single pair of dots, we now only need to check pairs along the hull, saving us a lot of time! As you grow older, learning about concepts like convex hulls becomes important in understanding various fields, including computer science, mathematics, engineering, and design. Just remember, a convex hull is simply the smallest shape that contains all given points, and it has many practical applications in simplifying complex problems! Did you notice that he was interested in any set of toys? Hello young learners! Today, we are going to explore a fun concept in mathematics called "modular arithmetic" or "clock arithmetic." This is like telling time on a clock, where we only care about the remainder when dividing by a certain number (like 12 for hours or 60 for minutes). We will use this idea to find remainders quickly and easily! Imagine you have a really big number, let's say $1234^{5678}$. Now, your task is to find the remainder when dividing this huge number by another number, say 13. You may think this would take forever with pencil and paper, but don't worry-with some clever tricks, we can make this process much faster! First, notice that $1234 \equiv 12 \equiv -1 (\text{mod } 13)$. In other words, when we divide $1234$ by $13$, the remainder is $-1$. Similarly, since $5678$ is an even number, raising any integer to an even power will result in the remainder being $1$ when divided by $13$. That means $(-1)^{5678} \equiv 1 (\text{mod } 13)$, making our final answer simply $1$. Here's how you could explain this to your friends using clocks: Imagine having two clocks, one showing the hour hand at $12$ ($12$ o'clock) and another one with the hour hand pointing towards $-1$ (which isn't possible on regular clocks, but just imagine it for now). Since both hands are pointing opposite directions, ask your friend which direction do all the numbers go if you turn each clock wheel once? They should say that the hour hand on the $12$-o'clock side moves forward one position, while the hour hand on the $-1$ side turns backward three positions. Now tell them that doing this rotation many times over still leaves us knowing exactly where both hour hands point without actually counting every single step. Just remember that turning the $12$-o'clock side always gives a positive movement, whereas the $-1$ side results in negative movements. In fact, we can apply these ideas to various real-world problems too! Let's say you wanted to share candies equally among 13 friends, but had way too many candies. By finding the remainder when distributing candies using modular arithmetic, you can instantly know how many extra candies you need to give out. So next time someone asks you to compute large remainders, surprise them with your quick thinking and mastery of modular arithmetic! Happy learning! Delete Duplicates in Microsoft Word: A Comprehensive Guide ====================================================== Microsoft Word is a powerful tool used by millions of people worldwide for various purposes such as creating documents, reports, letters, and resumes. However, one common issue that users face while working with large documents is duplicate content. This tutorial will guide you through the process of deleting duplicates in Microsoft Word using its built-in features. By following these steps, you can ensure your document is free from redundant information, making it easier to read and understand. Before we begin, let's discuss some essential tips and guidelines to keep in mind: * Always save a backup copy of your original document before attempting any changes. This way, if something goes wrong or if you need to revert to the previous version, you won't lose any important data. * Make sure to enable the 'Track Changes' feature before starting this process. It allows you to see what has been deleted and undo any unwanted modifications if necessary. You can find this option under the 'Review' tab. * Pay close attention to formatting when dealing with duplicates. Sometimes, two identical paragraphs may have different font sizes, styles, or indentation levels due to manual editing. Be consistent with your formatting choices after removing the duplicates. Step 1: Prepare Your Document ----------------------------- ### Step 1.1: Enable 'Show/Hide' Feature To start, click on the '\_' (paragraph) icon located at the top right corner of the Home tab or press Ctrl + Shift + 8 on your keyboard. Enabling this feature will display non-printing characters like spaces, tabs, line breaks, and paragraph marks. These symbols help identify potential issues related to spacing and formatting, which might cause false positives during the duplicate removal process. ![Enable Show/Hide](https://www.wikihow.com/images/thumb/a/ad/Find-and-Replace-Text-in-Word-2016-Step-3-Version-4.jpg/aid759835-v4- Camera typesThough there are many types of cameras, all include five indispensable components: (1) the camera box, which holds and protects the sensitive film from all light except that entering through the lens; (2) film, on which the image is recorded, a light-sensitive strip usually wound on a spool, either manually or automatically, as successive pictures are taken; (3) the light control, consisting of an aperture or diaphragm and a shutter, both often adjustable; (4) the lens, which focuses the light rays from the subject onto the film, creating the image, and which is usually adjustable by moving forward or back, changing the focus; and (5) the viewing system, which may be separate from the lens system (usually above it) or may operate through it by means of a mirror. Title: How to Download Wanted MIDI Music Introduction: MIDI (Musical Instrument Digital Interface) music files are a popular format for musicians and hobbyists alike due to their small size and flexibility. They contain musical notation rather than actual audio data, which allows for easy editing and customization. This tutorial will guide you through downloading wanted MIDI music using various methods and sources. Step 1: Identify the Song You Want as a MIDI File Before searching for your desired song in MIDI format, ensure that it is available. Websites like midiworld.com or vgmusic.com offer vast libraries of songs converted into MIDI files. However, if the specific arrangement isn't available, consider creating one yourself using software such as Synthesia or Anvil Studio. Key Tip: Be aware of copyright issues when sharing or distributing these files. Always respect artists' rights by obtaining necessary permissions beforehand. Step 2: Use Search Engines to Find Your Desired MIDI File Use search engines like Google with keywords including the name of the song, artist, and 'midi'. For example, "Bohemian Rhapsody Queen MIDI". The results may include direct links to websites hosting the file or forum discussions where users have shared them. Guideline: When using forums or peer-to-peer platforms, be cautious about potential viruses or malware. Only download from trusted sources. Step 3: Visit Specialized Websites for MIDI Files Websites dedicated solely to providing MIDI files often have more extensive collections and better organization compared to general-purpose sites. Examples include midisongwriter.co.uk, free-scores.com, and mutopiaproject.org. These sites also usually vet submissions for quality control. Key Tip: Some sites require registration; however, this can help prevent spam and provide a safer environment for downloading files. Step 4: Utilize Torrent Clients for Large Collections For those looking for large collections of MIDI files, torrent clients could be an option. Sites like The Pirate Bay or KickassTorrents might host .torrent files linking to entire databases of MIDI music. Remember though, downloading copyrighted material without permission is illegal. Guideline: Always check local laws regarding digital piracy and avoid engaging in unlawful activities. Step 5: Check File Integrity After Downloading After downloading any file, whether from a website or via P2P networks, run a virus scan immediately. Tools like Avast, Norton Antivirus, or Windows Defender can detect possible threats. If no threat is detected, proceed to open the file. Key Tip: Keep your antivirus software updated regularly for optimal protection against new viruses and malware. Step 6: Import the Downloaded MIDI File Into a Suitable Player or Software To listen to or edit the MIDI file, import it into a compatible program. Popular choices include Apple GarageBand, FL Studio, Ableton Live, or simply Windows Media Player depending on your needs and preferences. Conclusion: With patience and persistence, anyone can find their favorite tunes in MIDI format online. By following our simple steps, understanding associated risks, and applying recommended precautions, you too can enjoy listening to and manipulating your chosen pieces of music stored within compact, versatile MIDI files. Title: The Ultimate Guide to Applying the Best Floor Coating Introduction: Floor coating is an excellent way to protect and enhance the appearance of your concrete floors. This guide will walk you through the process of applying the best floor coating, ensuring professional results. **Materials Needed:** 1. Concrete floor 2. Shop vacuum or broom 3. Concrete etcher = = = children/CHIDES_NA/NewmanRatner/Interviews/07/7252PD.cha = = = What do I get on my plate? That's a crib made out of plastic. The oldest legal newspaper in Lithuania is the Polish Kurier Wilenski. It was first published in the 18th century and is now only popular with the Polish community. The biggest selling newspapers are: D' you need another piece of paper? The pressure and temperature of a gas are linked. As pressure goes up and down, so does temperature. Take a container that has a fixed amount of gas. A plunger seals one end of the container. A pressure gauge is attached, and a thermometer. Pushing in the plunger increases gas pressure. It forces the gas molecules into a smaller space. As a result, they move faster and make more impacts. This causes the gas to heat up. And the overall temperature of the gas rises too. An increase in pressure produces a rise in temperature. Pulling out the plunger reduces gas pressure. It gives the molecules more room to move. They make fewer impacts. The energy level of the molecules falls. And so does the temperature of the gas. A fall in pressure produces a fall in temperature. Now let's see what happens when it is temperature that changes first. When a gas is heated up, its molecules gain energy and start to move more quickly. They make more impacts. This increases the pressure the gas is exerting. Increasing the gas temperature produces increased pressure. Cooling has an opposite effect. The particles lose energy and slow down. They make fewer impacts. Pressure falls. So, for a fixed amount of gas, higher temperatures produce higher pressures. And lower temperatures produce lower pressures. This is put to use in compression-ignition or diesel engines. Air in the combustion chamber is highly compressed by the piston. This causes such a large rise in air temperature that when fuel is sprayed into it, the mixture ignites. Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Jacky. How? The horse said, " With a fight. See, if I fight against the pond with all of my strength, I will be able to get your helicopter out". Jacky was amazed and said, "Thanks a lot!" So the horse fought hard against the pond and in the end it was victorious. The helicopter was safe and sound! Jacky was so happy that she hugged the horse and thanked it. Title: How to Eat Sour Candy Introduction: Sour candy is a popular sweet treat that delivers a tangy, puckering sensation followed by a burst of flavor. This article will guide you through the process of eating sour candies with precision, ensuring maximum enjoyment while avoiding potential pitfalls. By following these steps, you'll be able to savor your favorite sour candies without any discomfort or mess. Step 1: Choose Your Preferred Sour Candy Select a sour candy of your choice. There are various types available, including sour patch kids, warheads, sour gummy worms, and more. The type of sour candy doesn't matter as much as its level of sourness; this determines how carefully you need to consume it. Key Tip: Always read the packaging before purchasing to ensure the candy meets your preferred level of tartness. Step 2: Check for Appropriate Consumption Age Ensure the chosen candy does not pose any choking hazards based on age recommendations listed on the package. Most sour candies are safe for children over three years old, but some may require adult supervision due to small parts or excessive chewiness. Guideline: If unsure about safety, consult an adult or refer to reputable online resources for guidance. Step 3: Wash Hands Thoroughly Before consuming sour candies (or any food), wash hands thoroughly using soap and warm water for at least 20 seconds. Proper hand hygiene reduces the risk of illness caused by germ transmission from hands to mouth. Step 4: Open the Package Carefully Open the sour candy wrapper slowly and deliberately. Ripping open the package too quickly can cause pieces of candy to fly out unexpectedly, potentially causing injury or loss of precious treats. Key Tip: Use both hands when opening the package - one to hold steady and the other to tear cautiously along the seal. Step 5: Place One Piece of Candy in Mouth Take only one piece of candy from the pack initially. Overloading your mouth could lead to difficulty breathing or speaking temporarily due to excess saliva production triggered by the sour taste. Step 6: Allow Salivation to Begin Naturally Let the sour coating dissolve naturally within your mouth. Resist the urge to bite down immediately, as doing so might release an intense burst of sourness all at once. Step 7: Gradually Chew Once Coating Dissolves Once the sour coating has mostly dissolved, begin gently chewing the rest of the candy. Make sure to maintain slow, deliberate movements to avoid accidentally biting off larger chunks than intended. Step 8: Swallow Safely When ready to swallow, take small sips of water if necessary to aid in the process. Never swallow large pieces of candy whole, as they can become stuck in the throat and cause coughing fits or choking. Step 9: Enjoy Additional Pieces Mindfully Repeat Steps 5-8 until satisfied. Remember to pace yourself and enjoy each piece individually to maximize flavor and minimize discomfort. Conclusion: By adhering to these simple yet essential steps, anyone can safely and comfortably indulge in their favorite sour candies. With practice, mindful consumption becomes second nature, enhancing overall satisfaction and reducing risks associated with hasty or careless snacking. Happy tasting! Universal Declaration of Human Rights This simplified version of the 30 Articles of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights has been created especially for young people. 1. We Are All Born Free & Equal. We are all born free. We all have our own thoughts and ideas. We should all be treated in the same way. 2. Don't Discriminate. These rights belong to everybody, whatever our differences. 3. The Right to Life. We all have the right to life, and to live in freedom and safety. 4. No Slavery. Nobody has any right to make us a slave. We cannot make anyone our slave. 5. No Torture. Nobody has any right to hurt us or to torture us. 6. You Have Rights No Matter Where You Go. I am a person just like you! 7. We're All Equal Before the Law. The law is the same for everyone. It must treat us all fairly. 8. Your Human Rights Are Protected by Law. We can all ask for the law to help us when we are not treated fairly. 9. No Unfair Detainment. Nobody has the right to put us in prison without good reason and keep us there, or to send us away from our country. 10. The Right to Trial. If we are put on trial this should be in public. The people who try us should not let anyone tell them what to do. 11. We're Always Innocent Till Proven Guilty. Nobody should be blamed for doing something until it is proven. When people say we did a bad thing we have the right to show it is not true. 12. The Right to Privacy. Nobody should try to harm our good name. Nobody has the right to come into our home, open our letters, or bother us or our family without a good reason. 13. Freedom to Move. We all have the right to go where we want in our own country and to travel as we wish. 14. The Right to Seek a Safe Place to Live. If we are frightened of being badly treated in our own country, we all have the right to run away to another country to be safe. 15. Right to a Nationality. The following sketch of his character by Polybius shows some of his distinguishing traits: _Scipio Aemilianus as a Sportsman_ After the war was decided, Paulus, in the belief that hunting was the best training and recreation that a young man could have, put the king's huntsmen at the orders of Scipio, and gave him full authority over everything connected with the chase. Scipio readily accepted the charge and, regarding it almost as a royal office, continued to occupy himself with it as long as the army remained in Macedonia after the battle. His youth and natural disposition qualified him for this pursuit, like a high-bred hound, and his devotion to hunting became permanent, being continued when he came to Rome and found Polybius as enthusiastic as himself. Consequently, all the time that other young men spent in the law-courts and with morning calls, waiting about in the Forum and trying thus to make a favourable impression on the people, was passed by Scipio in hunting; and as he was constantly performing brilliant and notable exploits, he distinguished himself more than all the rest. For they could not win credit except by injuring others; such are the conditions of legal action; but Scipio, without doing any harm to any one, gained a popular reputation for courage, matching words with deeds. AccessMyLibrary provides FREE access to millions of articles from top publications available through your library. Do you know what similes are? Maybe not, but you probably use them all the time. If you've ever said something is as sweet as honey or as hard as a rock, you've used a simile. A simile compares two unlike things and includes as or like. For example, when someone says "the dog's bark is as loud as a truck," he or she is using a simile to compare the dog's bark to the sound of a truck. Of attitudes in South America, a December 2003 survey found that 30% of Argentines thought that abortion should be allowed in Argentina "regardless of situation", 47% that it should be allowed "under some circumstances", and 23% that it should not be allowed "regardless of situation". A March 2007 poll about abortion in Brazil found that 65% of Brazilians believe that it "should not be modified", 16% that it should be expanded "to allow abortion in other cases", 10% that abortion should be "decriminalized", and 5% were "not sure". A July 2005 poll in Colombia found that 65.6% said they thought that abortion should remain illegal, 26.9% that it should be made legal, and 7.5% that they were unsure. She falls down] Rationale: This lesson teaches children about the long vowel correspondence i=/I//. In order to be able to read, children must recognize the different sounds vowels make because vowels are used to spell all words in our vocabulary. It is important for students to understand the phoneme and grapheme of each vowel. In this lesson, students will be able to identify the /i/ (short i) in spoken words, give it a meaningful name, and learn to spell words using the short I (/i/). Sentence strip with "Izzie the icky sticky iguana was inside and ill." Tin Man Fix-it (Educational Insights, phonics reader), Flash cards with the words: at, it, bit, did, hit, big, tin, kiss, lip, and swiss. Letter tiles I, t, b, d, h, g, n, k, l, p, 3 s's, w, a 1. Introduce the lesson by writing a lower case letter i on the board and ask the students if they know what sound the letter makes. "Does anyone know what sound this letter makes?"If students say correct sound and examples give students praise. "Awesome! the letter i make the /i/ sound!" Tell the students what they will be doing in today's activity. "Today we are going to learn how to identify the letter i and the /i/ sound in words." 2. Hold up the picture with the Icky Sticky hand gesture on it. Ask the students have they ever touched something that was sticky? "Have you ever touched icky, sticky glue or gum with your hands and you try to shake it off?" Well I have and it was very icky and sticky! "Do you hear the /i/ sound in icky-sticky?" If so I want you to say /i/ and shake your icky-sticky hands." Model and explain for the students how to do the icky-sticky hand gesture and how to move your mouth the correct way to make the /i/ sound. "When you say the /i/ sound and do your icky-sticky hand gesture, your mouth should be opened and you tongue slightly lowered, and then you should shake both your hands in and upward and downward motion. 3. "Let's take a look at a tongue twister (sentence strip). Izzie the icky sticky iguana was inside a igloo and ill Now let's all say it 3 times together. Good! Now every time we hear the sound /i/ lets stretch it out and say it loud. IIIzzie the iiiicky stiiicky iiiguana was iiiinside an iiigloo and iiiill. Good! Now let's say it one more time, but this time lets break the /i/ sound out of the word and say it separately. /I/zzie the /i/cky st/i/cky i/guana was /i/nside an /i/gloo and/i/ll." 4. Have the students to tell you if they hear the /i/ sound in different words. "I want to listen closely to some words as I say them, and tell me which words have the /i/ sound in them." "Do you hear the /i/ sound in big or bag? Pick or pack? Dig Stick or stack? Great job! You did hear the /i/ sound in big, pick, and stick." 5. Pass out primary paper and pencil to each student. "I want to be sure that everyone knows how to write the letter i." Demonstrate and encourage the students to write the letter i. Let students all try to write the letter i. "Start at the fence line and draw a straight tree top standing tall (have students draw line as you demonstrate on the board). Then go up a little over the tree and put a tiny round sun." 6. Begin the letterbox lesson. Pass out Letterbox sets and lower case letterbox tiles: I,t,b,d,h,g,n,k,l,p,3 s's, and w to each student. Demonstrate how to use the letterbox and letter tiles to pronounce and spell words with the students. "Now we are going to spell some words that have the /i/ sound in them. Remember that each box should only have one sound in it. I am going to spell the word "hip" watch and listen closely at what I do. H-h-h-h-i-i-i-i-p-p-p. It helps if you say the word very slowly to yourself. The first sound I hear is /h/, so I will put a h in the first box. Then I hear the /i/ sound that we have been learning about (model icky sticky hand gesture) so I will put an i in the second letterbox. Finally I hear the /p/ sound, so I will put the letter p in the third letterbox. Now let's see can we spell these words with the /i/ sound in them." Have the students use letterboxes and tiles to spell the following words [2 at, it 3bit did hit tin big kiss lip 4 Swiss]. Remember to tell the students how many boxes they will need for each word. "Now let's practice saying some words with the /i/ sound. See if you can use the steps that I used to spell hip, to spell the new words. When you are finished spelling a word, put a thumbs up in the air, and I will come by and look at your spelling." Then pass out the class set of index cards with the words it, bit, did, hit, big, tin, kiss, lip, and swiss . Have the students read the words on each card aloud as a class. 6. Pass out the book Tin Man Fix It to each student. Do a picture walk and book talk for the students, to get them interested in reading the book. "Today we are going to read Tin Man Fix It." This book is about a tin man named Tim and his friend Jim. They were playing outside when a big kid named Sid skates by and knocks Tim down. Tim falls and breaks apart. Will Jim be able to fix Tim?" Have the students read the text on their own. Afterwards have students to recall words with the /i/ sound that they read in the book and write those words on the board. "Who can tell me which words have the /i/ sound on page one." 7. For the assessment, I will give each student a worksheet that has picture that have the /i/ sound. Students will circle which sounds like /i/ and x out pictures that do not. "Wow, look at them go!" Tom says. "They are so fast and bright!" Anna says. They imagine that their rockets are going to different planets with strange animals and plants. They make up stories about what they see and do in space. But soon, the wind gets stronger and the sky gets darker. The rockets start to fall down and land on the ground. Did you eat your sandwich at school today or just your pie? Creating Borders in Adobe Photoshop Step 1: Open Photoshop Start by launching Adobe Photoshop on your computer. If you don't have it installed, download and install it first from the official Adobe website. Step 2: Create a New Document To create a new document, click on "File" in the top menu bar, then select "New." A dialog box will appear where you can set your preferred width, height, resolution, and background color. Click "Create" once done. Step 3: Import Image (Optional) If you want to add a border to an existing image, go to "File," choose "Open," navigate to your image file, and click "Open." Then drag and drop this layer below the blank layer created initially. Step 4: Select Canvas Size Ensure the blank layer is selected. Go to "Image" > "Canvas Size." This opens a window where you can adjust the canvas size. For adding equal borders around all sides, ensure 'Relative' is unchecked and input values under 'Width' and 'Height.' Check 'Center Content' if you wish to center the original content. Key Tip: To maintain aspect ratio while resizing, hold down the Shift key when changing dimensions. Step 5: Choose Foreground Color Decide what color you want your border to be. Go to the Tools panel, find the foreground color square (usually black), double-click it, and pick a color using the Color Picker window. Step 6: Draw Rectangle Marquee Tool Select the Rectangular Marquee Tool from the toolbar or press M on your keyboard. Position the cursor at one corner of where you want the border, left-click and drag diagonally across to the opposite corner creating a selection outline. Step 7: Fill Selection with Foreground Color With the selection still active, go to "Edit" > "Fill." Alternatively, use the shortcut Shift+F5. The Fill dialogue box appears; make sure 'Contents' says 'Color,' and that matches your chosen foreground color. Hit OK. Step 8: Deselect Deselect the selection area by going to "Select" > "Deselect" or pressing Ctrl+D (Windows) / Command+D (Mac). You should now see a colored rectangle representing your border. Step 9: Add Effects (Optional) You can further enhance your border by applying effects like stroke, inner glow, etc., found under Layer Style (double-click empty space beside layer name or right-click layer>Blending Options). Remember, less is often more regarding design - avoid overloading layers with unnecessary styles. Step 10: Save Your Work Once satisfied with your creation, save it appropriately depending on its intended purpose. Common formats include JPEG, PNG, or PSD (for editable files). To do so, head to "File" > "Save As..." and specify your preferences in the subsequent dialog boxes before clicking "Save." Remember, practice makes perfect. Don't hesitate to experiment with different settings and techniques as you get comfortable working within Photoshop! Okay would you like to have my change back? T oh yeah I'm doing it XXX. [walking toy cowboy across table to car] Title: How to Effectively Deal with Problems at Work Introduction: Workplace issues are inevitable, but learning how to handle them efficiently is crucial for your career growth and overall job satisfaction. This comprehensive guide will provide you with in-depth steps and valuable insights on dealing with problems at work while maintaining professionalism and productivity. Step 1: Identify the Problem The first step in addressing any issue at work is clearly identifying what the problem is. Reflect on recent events or interactions that may have caused stress, frustration, or discomfort. Be specific about who or what is involved, when and where it occurred, and why it's causing difficulty. Clearly defining the problem allows you to focus on finding an appropriate solution. Key Tip: Avoid jumping to conclusions; gather all necessary information before determining the root cause of the problem. Step 2: Assess the Impact Consider how this problem affects you, your colleagues, and the organization as a whole. Understanding the extent of its impact can help you prioritize your actions and decide whether it requires immediate attention or if it can be addressed over time. Guideline: Keep emotions in check during this assessment process to ensure objectivity. Step 3: Develop Potential Solutions Brainstorm possible ways to resolve the identified problem. Think creatively and consider various perspectives. List down as many solutions as possible without judging their feasibility at this stage. Key Tip: Engage trusted coworkers or mentors in this brainstorming session for fresh ideas and different viewpoints. Step 4: Evaluate Solution Options Review each potential solution and weigh its pros and cons. Consider factors such as resources available, timelines, and stakeholder interests. Select the most viable option(s) based on these evaluations. Guideline: Remember that there might not always be a perfect solution; instead, aim for one that brings about positive change while minimizing negative impacts. Step 5: Plan Your Approach Outline the steps needed to implement your chosen solution(s). Determine who needs to be involved, what resources are required, and when action should take place. Having a clear plan increases the likelihood of successful resolution. Key Tip: Anticipate obstacles and include contingency plans in your approach to maintain flexibility. Step 6: Communicate with Relevant Parties Discuss your concerns and proposed solution(s) with relevant individuals, including those directly affected by the problem and those who can assist in resolving it. Use assertive communication techniques to express yourself effectively and professionally. Guideline: Listen actively to others' opinions and remain open to feedback or alternative suggestions. Step 7: Implement the Chosen Solution Carry out the planned actions according to the schedule outlined in your approach. Monitor progress regularly and make adjustments as necessary. Key Tip: Maintain documentation of all communications, meetings, and changes related to the problem resolution process for future reference. Step 8: Review Outcome & Learn from Experience Once the implemented solution has had enough time to take effect, evaluate its success in addressing the initial problem. Identify lessons learned throughout the process and determine areas for improvement moving forward. Guideline: Celebrate achievements, no matter how small, to boost morale and motivation. Conclusion: Addressing workplace problems promptly and constructively contributes significantly to a harmonious working environment. By following these eight steps, you demonstrate maturity, leadership, and commitment to fostering positive change within your organization. We may height pull out the food at home buddy. Don't you like miss Moore the best? That's alright I'll get the wipes out. Title: How to Clean a Blood Stain from Car Upholstery ===================================================== Blood stains on car upholstery can be tricky to remove due to their protein-based nature. However, with the right approach and materials, you can effectively eliminate these stains. This tutorial will guide you through the process using household items and professional cleaning products. Follow these steps carefully to restore your car's interior to its original condition. ### Things You'll Need * Cold water * White cloth or paper towels * Mild dish soap (without bleach) * Soft-bristle brush (optional) * Absorbent powder (baking soda, cornstarch, or talcum powder) * Vacuum cleaner * Enzymatic cleaner (such as Nature's Miracle or Kids 'N' Pets) * Rubbing alcohol (70% isopropyl alcohol) * Hydrogen peroxide (3% solution) ### Step 1: Blot the stain with cold water Well you look and get one out of all this mess. where's the eyes of the teddy bear? AROUND AND AROUND (THE WATER CYCLE) Grades 2 - 3 The water cycle? What is it? This lesson introduces students to the major components and vocabulary of the water cycle through use of video. It culminates with the class making a water cycle in a and discussing care of our water. "Take a Look #118: Rain" Students will be able to: - Demonstrate knowledge of the components of the water cycle by it and by making a model of it. - Explain how the Earth-Sun relationship shapes climate. - Understand the reasons and explain why we need to take care of our For each student: - 10 clear cup - 1/3 cup (measuring cup) - empty ice cube tray - small clear jar filled with rocks - hot plate & tea kettle - frozen ice cubes in tray - plant in a baggie - dry erase markers - quart-size zip-lock baggie - duplicated picture of water cycle - permanent markers and crayons - small amount of gravel - 1 sheet of drawing paper - water cycle Who has heard of something called the water cycle? We call this a pretest so I can have some idea of what we learn today. So please, draw the water cycle for me." Pass out and crayons and have the class draw the water cycle as best they can. Remember our lesson about the three states of matter: solid, liquid, and gas?" Allude to a previously covered topic by asking the class to what those three states of matter are. A solid is something that does not change shape when moved from place to place. A liquid is something that does not have a shape of its own. It can be poured. Water is a liquid. A gas is something that does not have a shape of its own and expands to a container. Air is a gas. "Today we are going to be talking about water and the water cycle. A cycle is like a circle. It goes around and around. We have what is a closed system on Earth. All of the water here stays here. All the water that we have on Earth today is water that was here when the dinosaurs here. None of it escapes into space. Some of this water on Earth is for us to use as fresh water and some isn't. Let's pretend that I can shrink all the water on Earth into these 10 cups of water here. This represents all the water available to us on Measure 1/3 cup of water from the 10 cups. "This 1/3 cup represents all the freshwater on Earth. Freshwater is water that we can drink." "The remaining 9 2/3 cups of water is salt water." Pour salt that water. Set aside. To visualize frozen freshwater, I will remove 12 teaspoons from the 1/3 cup of fresh water and pour this into an ice tray. This shows the world's fresh water that is frozen in polar ice caps and glaciers." Set "We can't use this water, either." "To show fresh ground water, I am going to pour all but 1 teaspoon of the remaining freshwater into a clear jar containing rocks. The water in this jar represents the amount of water that occurs in the ground. The remaining 1 teaspoon represents all the fresh surface water in the This is all the water we have available to us and we need to take care of it." (This is an adaptation from GEE-WOW!
