Rationale: For students to learn to read, they must first recognize what each correspondence says. It is important that students learn vowel correspondences first. Learning letters and their correspondences will lead to successful decoding, reading fluency, and reading comprehension. For this lesson, students will learn to recognize the letter u and the correspondence u=/u/. *primary paper and pencil for each student *letter boxes (Elkonin Boxes) *plastic letters (one set for each child) h, u, n, t, l, c, b, o, k, j, p, s, r, g, c, h, i *poster with tongue twister printed on it -Tongue twister: Uncle Ugh was upset because he was unable to put his umbrella up. *Copy of the book Bud the Sub one for each student *Which word has /u/ in it worksheet (attached). *9 large pieces of cardstock with the following words written on them: (4) hunt, lunch, block, junk, plus (5) strung, crunch, crust, blink (1)To engage the students, talk about how the alphabet is our "code" for spoken language. Explain to the students that we will be learning all about the code and the sound for the letter u. Example: Today students, we will be learning the all about the code for the letter u. Talk with the students about how whenever you say u's sound, /u/ your mouth is open about half way and your tongue stays behind your bottom teeth. Have the students say this with you. Example: I want you all to try this with me. Let's say /u/ all together on the count of three. These are some words that have /u/ in them. Model reading the words scrub, tub, truck, and grunt. Teacher should put emphasis on the /u/ sounds in the words. (2)Students will repeat the tongue twister with me and act as if they are punching themselves in the stomach whenever they hear or say the /u/. Uncle Ugh was upset because he was unable to put his umbrella up. (3)Pass out the primary paper and pencils to everyone. Explain to the students that we will be learning how to write the "code" for the letter u. (The students should already know the names for the lines on the primary paper, however they may need a review). Example: Students, to write a u on our paper, we are going to start at the fence dip down to the sidewalk and come back up to the fence. Then on the right side of your dip, start at the fence and drop all the way down to the sidewalk again. As the teacher is verbally explaining this, s/he should also be modeling on the board where a primary line should be drawn. After everyone has done it together, have the students try it themselves by finishing out the line with u's. (4)Next, we will be learning to spell and read some words that have the /u/sound in them. At this point the teacher needs to take up the primary paper and pencils and distribute the letter boxes and the letters to the students. Teacher will model how to spell and read the words crunch, strung, and scrub. Example: Boys and girls, this is how I would spell the word crunch. Since it has 5 sounds, we need 5 letter boxes. In the first letter box I would put a c for /c/. In the second LB, I would put the r for /r/. In the third LB, I would put u for /u/. In the fourth LB I would put the n for /n/. Finally in the last letter box, I would put both c and h for /ch/. Students will spell each of the words listed in the materials list in their letter boxes one at a time with the teacher coming around to make sure that they are correctly spelled. The letter boxes and the letters should be taken away while the students attempt to read the words that the teacher will be holding in the front of the room (these are the cardstocks that have the /u/ words written on them). Students will choral read the words making the punching gesture to their stomachs. (5) The teacher then needs to take up the letter boxes and the letters and distribute the copies of Bud the Sub. Teacher does a book talk. Example: Bud the sub has a boss named Gus. Gus guides Bud into a tug on accident. Uh oh! What will happen to Bud? You'll have to read to find out. Students will read the book with a partner. Again the teacher will be walking around making sure that everyone is on task. As a review, the teacher will have the students coral read the book and then talk about all the words that have /u/ in them. (6) Finally, as a review the teacher will talk about what the students learned in today's lesson. Example: I like the way that everyone participated in the lesson today. We talked about u=/u/. What is our gesture for u=/u/? (Students should remember the punching gesture to the stomach). For independent practice and the assessment for this lesson, students will complete a worksheet that has them draw a line from the picture to the word. Amy Crump Uh...Did I Do That? http://www.auburn.edu/academic/education/reading_genie/persp/crumpbr.html Vowels: Bud the Sub (1990). Carson, CA (USA) St. Albans, Herts (UK): to Navigations Index Adam what's your dirty undershirt doing in here? Imagine you have a jar filled with 100 blue marbles (type A). These blue marbles slowly turn into red marbles (type B) over time. At the same time, these red marbles gradually disappear. Let's call the number of blue marbles at any given time Na and the number of red marbles Nb. We want to understand how fast the number of blue and red marbles changes as time passes. To do this, we need to figure out two things: 1. How quickly the blue marbles become red marbles. 2. How quickly the red marbles disappear. First, let's consider the blue marbles. Since there are initially 100 blue marbles and some will change into red marbles over time, the total number of marbles must decrease. We can say that the rate at which the number of blue marbles decreases is proportional to its current count. So, the equation would be dNa/dt = - Na/Ta where dNa/dt represents the change in the number of blue marbles over time, Na is the current number of blue marbles, and Ta is a constant representing the average time taken for one marble to change color. Next, let's examine the red marbles. They appear when blue marbles transform, so their creation rate is equal to the disappearance rate of blue marbles or Na/Ta. However, red marbles also vanish over time, so we should account for that too. This behavior is represented by another constant, Tb, similar to Ta. Therefore, the equation for the red marbles becomes dNb/dt = Na/Ta - Nb/Tb, where dNb/dt indicates the change in the number of red marbles with respect to time. In summary, understanding how different groups of items interact and change with time requires examining each group individually while considering both appearance and disappearance rates. In our example, the interaction between blue and red marbles shows us how radioactive decays work through easily relatable concepts! He spoke quietly--for Mr. Crane was never one to bestow praise cheaply--concerning the work which Ward was doing, and succeeded in drawing from the troubled lad so many of his opinions on matters pertaining to his home life and experiences in Rockford, that in spite of himself Ward felt his anger and mortification disappearing for the time, and was soon feeling quite at his ease. Today, let's talk about a fun puzzle involving imaginary boxes and balls. Don't worry, this isn't about lifting or moving heavy things; instead, we will use our minds to explore some interesting ideas. Imagine you have two types of boxes, labeled "$b$" and "$c$". Each box can hold an infinite number of small balls. The challenge is to find a way to put these balls together using simple rules. Let's call the total number of balls when we just add the boxes "plus" ($b+c$), and the total number of balls when we combine them by filling one box after another "times" ($b o c$). Here's where it gets tricky: we imagine that these infinite boxes follow a special rule called $a^2=a$. This means that no matter how many balls are in a box, if we duplicate the balls in that box (which would give us $a^2$) we still end up with the same number of balls as before (just like $a$). Sounds strange, doesn't it?! But remember, this is just a pretend game. Now, here's where your fantastic brainpower comes in. Using our rule ($a^2=a$), can you figure out a clever way to show that putting the balls together "plus" is actually the same as combining them "times"? It may seem impossible, but let's see how far we can get! You started by writing down the equation: $b+c=(b+c)^2=b^2+2bc+c^2=b+2bc+c.$ However, now you're stuck. That's okay! Puzzling through problems helps our brains grow stronger. So, how can we continue? Well, think about those extra '$2bc$' balls. Can we distribute them between the '$b$' and '$c$' boxes while keeping our magical $a^2=a$ rule intact? What happens if we do that? Give it a try and see where it leads you! Remember, there's no need to rush-enjoy the process and learn something new along the way. Happy puzzling! Title: How to Buy a Horse Grooming Kit Buying a horse grooming kit is an essential task for any horse owner or enthusiast. A well-stocked grooming kit can help ensure your horse's health, comfort, and appearance. This article will provide you with a comprehensive guide on how to buy a horse grooming kit, complete with detailed steps, helpful tips, and crucial information. **Step 1: Understand the Purpose of a Horse Grooming Kit** Horse grooming kits are designed to maintain a horse's coat, skin, hooves, and overall hygiene. Regular grooming also allows you to check for cuts, lumps, parasites, or other issues that may require veterinary attention. Moreover, grooming strengthens the bond between horses and their handlers by providing opportunities for mutual trust and relaxation. **Step 2: Identify Necessary Items for Your Grooming Kit** A basic horse grooming kit should include: * Curry comb (for loosening dirt and hair) * Stiff-bristled body brush (for removing loose hair and dirt after currying) * Soft-bristled finishing brush (for final polishing) * Mane and tail comb (for detangling manes and tails without causing breakage) * Hoof pick (for cleaning out the hoof) * Sponge or cloth (for wiping down face and sensitive areas) * Dandy brush or shedding blade (optional but useful during shedding season) **Step 3: Choose Quality Tools** Investing in high-quality tools ensures durability and effectiveness over time. Low-grade materials might save money upfront but could lead to frequent replacement costs and potential harm to your horse if they break or become rough during use. Look for brushes made from natural fibers such as boar bristles or rubber curries instead of plastic ones which tend to be harsher on the horse's skin. **Step 4: Consider Size and Portability** If you frequently travel with your horse, consider purchasing a compact or portable grooming kit. Many companies offer foldable versions containing all necessary tools. Alternatively, individual items can be stored in a sturdy bag or box for easy transportation. **Step 5: Purchase From Reputable Suppliers** To guarantee safety and product longevity, always purchase from trusted equine supply stores or reputable online retailers specializing in horse equipment. Reading customer reviews and checking return policies before buying can prevent future disappointments. **Key Tips and Guidelines:** * Always approach grooming gently, especially around sensitive areas like the eyes, ears, and belly. Never force a scared or uncomfortable horse into being groomed; this could damage both your relationship and cause unnecessary stress. * Replace worn-out tools promptly. Frayed bristles or broken combs can scratch or irritate your horse's skin. * Keep separate sets of brushes for different horses if possible to avoid spreading disease or parasites. * Clean your brushes regularly. After every few uses, remove excess hair and wash them thoroughly with mild soap and warm water. Allow them to dry completely before using again. * Remember that while some products claim to reduce grooming time significantly, nothing beats manual care when building rapport and ensuring thorough cleanliness. By following these steps and guidelines, you will create a suitable horse grooming kit catered to your needs and those of your horse. Not only does regular grooming promote physical wellbeing, but it also offers precious moments of connection between human and animal companions. Title: How to Choose Diet and Fitness Apps Introduction: With countless diet and fitness apps available today, selecting the right one can be overwhelming. However, choosing an appropriate app tailored to your goals can significantly improve motivation, track progress, and enhance overall health outcomes. This wiki-style guide will walk you through essential steps to choose a suitable diet and fitness application that aligns with your objectives and preferences. Step 1: Define Your Goals Before diving into various options, clarify what you hope to achieve from using a diet or fitness app. Common objectives include weight loss, muscle gain, improving cardiovascular endurance, maintaining current fitness levels, managing medical conditions like diabetes, etc. Having clear targets makes it easier to find applications designed specifically for these purposes. Key Tip: Be realistic about your goals; drastic changes often lead to frustration and quick abandonment of plans. Gradual modifications are more sustainable over time. Step 2: Identify Key Features Different users require different functionalities depending upon their lifestyle and personal habits. Some common features include meal planning, calorie tracking, workout routines, activity monitoring, water intake reminders, sleep tracking, social networking capabilities, etc. Prioritize which ones matter most to you. Guideline: Consider factors such as whether you prefer home workouts or gym sessions, if you need help creating balanced meals, or if you're interested in connecting with friends for mutual support and encouragement. Step 3: Check Compatibility With Devices Ensure that the chosen app is compatible with your device(s). Most popular apps cater to both iOS and Android platforms, but some may only operate effectively on specific operating systems or versions. Also, check if they integrate well with other devices like smartwatches or heart rate monitors that you might own. Step 4: Review User Interface & Ease Of Use A user-friendly interface enhances adherence to any software product. Look out for clean layouts, easy navigation, intuitive controls, and readable font sizes. Remember, complexity does not necessarily equate to effectiveness. An unnecessarily complicated design could hinder usage rather than facilitate it. Step 5: Read Reviews & Ratings User reviews provide valuable insights into an app's performance, reliability, customer service, bugs, updates, and overall user experience. Pay attention to feedback related to accuracy (especially important for nutrition information), responsiveness, data privacy policies, and technical issues. Step 6: Assess Scientific Basis & Credentials While many apps claim scientific backing, not all live up to this promise. Research the developers' credentials, partnerships with reputed institutions, peer-reviewed publications featuring the app, or endorsements by certified professionals. Steer clear of fad diets or unsustainable exercise programs disguised under fancy interfaces. Step 7: Test Drive Multiple Options Most diet and fitness apps offer free trials or basic versions. Utilize these opportunities to test several options before committing financially. Evaluate compatibility with your routine, enjoyment level during usage, and perceived benefits during this trial period. Step 8: Consult Healthcare Providers If dealing with pre-existing medical conditions or starting a new regimen after prolonged sedentary behavior, consult healthcare providers before incorporating any diet or fitness app into your daily life. They can recommend safe practices and reliable resources suited to individual needs. Conclusion: Selecting the perfect diet and fitness app requires careful consideration of personal goals, preferred features, device compatibility, usability, credibility, real-world experiences, and professional advice. By following this comprehensive guide, you increase chances of finding a helpful companion towards achieving improved health and wellness. Chi go wash your hands and come eat. Title: How to Convert BMP to JPEG: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: This tutorial will guide you through the process of converting bitmap (BMP) image files to joint photographic experts group (JPEG) format using various software options available both online and offline. Converting from BMP to JPEG is beneficial when working with large-sized files or sharing them over the internet as JPEGs are more compact than their BMP counterparts without compromising much quality due to lossy compression. Key Tips & Guidelines: 1. Understand that during conversion, there might be some loss of detail depending upon the level of compression used. However, this difference may not be noticeable unless viewed at high magnification levels. 2. Always keep an original copy of your BMP file before starting the conversion process. This ensures that if anything goes wrong, you can revert back to the original version. 3. Be aware that certain features unique to BMP such as transparency won't carry over into the converted JPEG format since JPEG does not support alpha channels. 4. While most modern applications provide excellent results, always review your output after conversion to ensure satisfactory quality. Step 1: Choose Your Software Multiple tools exist for converting BMP files to JPEG including dedicated desktop apps, web services, and built-in functionalities within popular operating systems like Windows and macOS. For our example, we'll use an easy-to-access free tool - Online-Convert.com. Remember, choosing reputable platforms prevents potential security risks associated with uploading sensitive data online. Step 2: Access The Online Conversion Tool Open your preferred browser and navigate to www.online-convert.com. From its homepage, locate and click on the "Image Convert" option located under the "Convert To" section. Step 3: Select File Type & Upload Image In the new page displayed, select "Convert to JPG". Click on the "Choose Files" button and browse your computer for the target BMP image(s). Alternatively, drag and drop the files directly onto the designated area. Step 4: Customize Options (Optional) Although optional, customizing settings allows fine-tuning the final product based on specific needs. You can adjust parameters such as Quality Level (affects size vs quality tradeoff), Color Depth, Smoothing Filter etc., but leaving these defaults often yields good enough results. Step 5: Initiate Conversion Process Once satisfied with all selections, initiate the conversion by clicking the "Start Conversion" button. Wait while your BMP file is uploaded and processed. Larger files take longer time. Step 6: Download Converted File After successful completion, download links will appear. Click the "Download" link corresponding to your newly converted JPEG image. Save the file to your desired location on your local device. Step 7: Verify Successful Conversion Review the downloaded JPEG file visually to confirm correct conversion. Use any default photo viewer application installed on your system. Check whether details are preserved and no unwanted alteration occurred. By following these steps meticulously, one should successfully convert BMP files to JPEG format regardless of prior experience. Keep experimenting with different tools and methods until comfortable achieving optimal outcomes consistently. Don't munch up and bring 'em to me. oh, there's a cup, we can have a drink. Title: How to Care for a Pet Rock When You Are Ill Pet rocks are low-maintenance pets that can bring joy and serenity into your life. Even when you're feeling under the weather, taking care of your pet rock can be a soothing activity. This guide will provide detailed steps on how to care for your pet rock while ill, ensuring both you and your rock have a healthy and happy relationship. ### Step 1: Create a Designated Space *Designate a specific area in your home for your pet rock.* This space should ideally be quiet, clean, and free from disturbances. A designated area ensures your pet rock remains safe and secure while you focus on recovering. Key tip: Choose an elevated surface to prevent dust accumulation and reduce the risk of insect infestation. ### Step 2: Clean the Rock Regularly *Clean your pet rock with mild soap and warm water.* Although pet rocks do not produce waste or get sick like other pets, cleaning them regularly prevents dirt buildup and maintains their appearance. Use a soft brush to gently scrub the rock, rinse thoroughly, and let it air dry completely before returning it to its designated space. Guideline: Clean your pet rock once a week to maintain optimal hygiene. ### Step 3: Protect Your Pet Rock from Extreme Temperatures *Keep your pet rock away from heaters, air conditioners, and direct sunlight.* Exposure to extreme temperatures may cause your pet rock to crack or develop mineral deposits. Instead, find a comfortable spot in your home where the temperature is consistently moderate. Tip: If you must place your pet rock near a window, ensure it is shaded during peak sun hours. ### Step 4: Handle With Care *Avoid handling your pet rock excessively,* especially if you're experiencing tremors or weakness due to illness. Overhandling might lead to scratches or chips on the rock's surface. Limit interaction to gentle placement in its designated space and occasional light touching to avoid causing any damage. ### Step 5: Monitor Humidity Levels *Ensure the humidity levels around your pet rock remain stable.* Fluctuations in humidity could result in unwanted mineral growths. To monitor humidity, consider using a hydrometer. Maintain indoor humidity between 40% and 60%. If necessary, invest in a humidifier or dehumidifier to control the environment around your pet rock. ### Step 6: Prevent Pest Infestations *Inspect your pet rock routinely for signs of pests such as ants or termites.* While less likely than with live pets, pests can still affect pet rocks. Should you notice any signs of infestation, clean your pet rock immediately with a mixture of equal parts white vinegar and water. Rinse well and allow it to dry completely before placing it back in its designated space. ### Step 7: Display Artistically *Decorate the area around your pet rock to create a visually appealing display.* Add plants, stones, or artwork to enhance the ambiance. Not only does this contribute to your recovery by creating a calming atmosphere, but it also adds personality to your pet rock's living space. Remember to keep decorative items at a safe distance from the rock to prevent scratches or damages. By following these steps, you can successfully care for your pet rock even while ill. The primary goal is maintaining a consistent routine and providing a stable, clean environment for your beloved rock friend. Well see we cut it up before we give it to him. Today, let's explore the exciting world of counting and probability through a fun problem involving 5-digit numbers. Imagine you have a task to create a 5-digit number using digits from 0 to 9, but there's a catch - you cannot start your number with 0 or 1! How many unique 5-digit numbers can you make? Let's dive into this thrilling adventure together! First, we know that our five-digit number must not begin with zero since it would then become a four-digit number. So, for the first digit, we have eight options available (the remaining digits except for 0 and 1). Now, for the next four positions, any digit from 0 to 9 can be used without any restrictions. As there are ten possibilities for each spot, let us calculate the total amount of distinct numbers we could form. To find out, simply multiply these quantities together using the counting rule: Total combinations = First digit options Second, Third, Fourth & Fifth digit options. In our case, that means: Total combinations = 8 (first digit) 10 (second digit) 10 (third digit) 10 (fourth digit) 10 (fifth digit) By calculating all those products, do you know how many exclusive 5-digit numbers you can craft under these conditions? It turns out to be a whopping 80,000 unique combinations! That's enough numbers to give every student in a massive school their very own personalized identification code! Remember, learning math concepts like counting doesn't always mean working on boring sums; instead, it opens doors to understanding intriguing problems just like this one. Keep exploring mathematical ideas, and who knows what fantastic questions await your curious mind! Happy counting! No uh one thing we did was saying once upon a time though. Track and field events take place in a sports stadium, either on the running track, or on the field inside the running track. Stretched see it's warped like our Candy land puzzle just a minute. [aft runs to other side of room] Ancient people were afraid of comets - they were said to bring bad luck or foretell disaster. We now know that comets are small ice worlds that have been left over from the formation of the planets. Billions of comets are thought to exist on the edge of the Solar System, beyond the orbit of Pluto. Most comets are so small and so far away that we cannot see them, even in the biggest telescopes. But we can see them when they head inwards toward the Sun and grow tails of gas and dust. (The word comet means 'hairy star'.) The heat of the Sun makes the ices turn into gas that streams for millions of kilometres into space. Despite their length, the dust particles they contain are no bigger than grains of sand. Some comets take only a few years to swing around the Sun. Some - like Halley's comet - take much longer. Others appear once and are never seen again. Now every one who lives in the country is perfectly familiar with those little disturbances that often arise all of a sudden among sparrows, when about half a dozen go flying into a bush together, squabbling, bickering, and fighting with fearful ferocity. Creating a lasting tradition for baby's first Christmas is a wonderful way to start family holiday customs that can be passed down through generations. This guide will provide you with detailed steps on how to create meaningful and memorable traditions for your little one's inaugural yuletide season. ### Step 1: Brainstorm Ideas as a Family Key Tip: Including all members of the household in the brainstorming process ensures buy-in and commitment from everyone involved. Start by setting aside some time for a family meeting where you discuss potential ideas for your new Christmas tradition. Consider factors such as age appropriateness, budget constraints, available resources, and personal preferences when evaluating different concepts. Encourage creativity and open-mindedness during this discussion to foster an environment conducive to generating unique and engaging traditions. ### Step 2: Research Existing Traditions Guideline: Familiarize yourself with popular existing Christmas traditions to gain inspiration and insight into what has worked well for other families. To help spark your imagination, research various established Christmas traditions from around the world. Some examples include: * The Norwegian custom of hiding a pickle ornament within the Christmas tree for children to find on Christmas morning. * The German practice of Advent calendars counting down the days until December 25th. * The British tradition of enjoying a special meal on Christmas Eve known as "The Feast of Seven Fishes." Incorporate elements from these traditions or adapt them to suit your family's needs and interests. ### Step 3: Select Meaningful Activities Key Tip: Choose activities that hold significance for your family and resonate with your values and beliefs. Narrow down your list of potential traditions to those that align closely with your family's interests, values, and spiritual practices. For instance, if religious observance is important to you, consider incorporating nativity scene building or attending church services into your festivities. Alternatively, focus on secular activities like cookie decorating, crafting homemade gifts, or watching classic holiday movies together. ### Step 4: Establish a Budget Guideline: Determine financial limitations early in the planning process to avoid overspending and ensure sustainability of the tradition. Decide how much money you are willing to allocate towards implementing your chosen traditions. Be realistic about your finances and remember that creating memories does not have to involve excessive spending. Opt for low-cost or no-cost options whenever possible, such as making handmade ornaments or volunteering at a local charity organization. ### Step 5: Create a Schedule Key Tip: Clearly outline when each tradition will take place to manage expectations and facilitate smooth execution. Once you have selected your traditions and determined your budget, create a schedule detailing when each activity will occur throughout the month of December leading up to Christmas Day. Share this calendar with all family members so they know what to expect and can plan accordingly. ### Step 6: Gather Supplies Guideline: Collect necessary materials ahead of time to minimize stress and enable seamless implementation of traditions. Assemble any required supplies for your chosen traditions well before their scheduled dates. This may include items such as ornament-making kits, ingredients for baking cookies, or crafting materials for DIY gift creation. By gathering everything you need in advance, you can eliminate last-minute rushing and enjoy a more relaxed experience. ### Step 7: Document the Process D' you think that's what it is? Subject: Fun Math Practice Straight to Your Inbox! Hey there Grade-Schoolers! Get ready for a daily math challenge delivered straight to your mailbox. These won't be just any easy addition or multiplication problems-they'll be brain teasers designed to make your mind work harder. And don't worry, no need to study electromagnetism or integration yet (you're still in grade school after all). Here's why practicing tougher problems can help you out: Imagine you're working on a puzzle. At first glance, it seems impossible! But then you start trying different pieces together, even those that seem unrelated. Gradually but surely, a picture begins to emerge. That feeling when everything clicks into place? That's exactly what solving these challenging math questions feels like! Plus, it's fun! Starting today, every day at around 12:00 PM noon, we'll send you one head-scratcher problem. Don't stress if you don't get it right away-these are tricky by design. Take your time, think creatively, and give it your best shot. Who knows, maybe you'll figure it out before bedtime! And guess what? Even if you don't solve it immediately, seeing the solution later will still benefit you. Over time, you'll notice patterns and strategies that can help you tackle similar problems down the road. So keep an eye on those emails because each one comes with its own unique surprise inside. Creating new folders in Launchpad on Mac OS X Lion is an easy way to organize your applications and make them more accessible. By grouping similar apps together in folders, you can quickly find what you need without having to search through numerous pages of icons. Here's a comprehensive guide on how to create new folders in Launchpad on Mac OS X Lion: 1. **Open Launchpad:** Click on the icon that looks like a rocket ship, usually located in your Dock. If you don't see it there, you can also open Launchpad by pressing F4 (on some keyboards) or swiping up with three fingers on your trackpad. This will bring up the main screen displaying all installed applications. Key tip: Organizing your applications starts with knowing where they are. Make sure you have explored and familiarized yourself with Launchpad before proceeding. 2. **Find two applications you want to put into a folder:** Identify which apps you would like to keep close at hand by browsing through the different pages available in Launchpad. For example, let's say we want to create a folder containing both Safari and Google Chrome web browsers. 3. **Press and hold an application icon until they start jiggling:** Similar to moving apps around on your iPhone or iPad home screens, press firmly on one app icon until all icons begin shaking. You should notice small 'x' marks appearing on top of some app icons; these represent uninstall options and aren't relevant to our current task. Guideline: Keep in mind that while the icons are jiggling, only certain actions can be performed - creating folders being one of them. 4. **Drag one app over another:** Now that the apps are jiggling, pick up the first app you decided to include in the folder (in this case, Safari), then drag it onto the second app (Google Chrome). Release the mouse button or lift your finger off the trackpad once you see a gray rectangle surrounding both icons. 5. **Name your new folder:** After releasing the dragged app, you'll notice a newly created folder containing both apps. A default name such as "Web" will appear above the folder. To change the name, click directly on the text inside the folder preview and type in your preferred label. Press Enter or Return when done editing. 6. **Add additional apps to the folder:** If you decide later that you want to add more apps to this folder, simply locate the desired app within Launchpad, enter jiggle mode again by pressing and holding its icon, then drag it into the existing folder. The folder will automatically update to reflect the added app. 7. **Exit jiggle mode:** Once you've finished organizing your apps into folders, press the Escape key on your keyboard or wait a few seconds for the icons to stop shaking. Your changes will be saved automatically. Creating new folders in Launchpad on Mac OS X Lion allows you to streamline your workflow, making it easier than ever to access frequently used applications. With just a bit of practice, you'll have all your favorite tools neatly categorized and ready for action! And I don't know that he actually recognizes it... Firefighters and police use those numbers to find houses in an emergency so they can help you. The number you see on the front of your house is your home's first name. The street name or number is your home's last name. The home's first name and last name together make up your address. This house's first name is: 5677 (first) Piringer Street SW (last). This lets light come in through the aperture and make a picture on the film or electronic sensor. In many cameras, the size of the aperture can be changed to let in more light or less light. The amount of time that the shutter lets light through can be changed as well. This also lets in more light or less light. Most of the time, electronics inside the camera control these, but in some cameras the person taking the picture can change them as well. [Disney Phone says "this is Pinocchio calling"] Title: Change Your Minecraft Skin in 60 Seconds Introduction: In this tutorial, you will learn how to change your Minecraft skin quickly and easily. This process can be done within one minute once you've familiarized yourself with the steps. A custom skin allows you to personalize your Minecraft character, making your gameplay experience unique. Follow these simple, illustrated steps to switch up your look! Difficulty: Easy Time Required: Less than 1 minute List of Tools Needed: - Computer or mobile device with internet access - Mojang account login credentials (email address and password) Instructions: Step 1: Access the Minecraft website Visit <https://www.minecraft.net/> using your preferred web browser. Log into your Mojang account by clicking on 'Sign in' at the top right corner of the page. Enter your email address and password associated with your Minecraft account when prompted. Once logged in, click on the profile icon and select 'Profile.' Key Tips & Guidelines: - Double-check that the URL is correct; phishing sites may resemble the official site but have slightly different addresses. - Keep your login information secure and private. Step 2: Navigate to your Profile Page After logging in successfully, you should see your user profile displayed on the screen. If not, ensure you are signed in before proceeding to the next step. Here, you can view basic details like your username, date joined, transaction history, and linked accounts. Step 3: Select 'Skins' tab On your profile page, locate the tabs near the center of the page. These include 'Details,' 'Payment,' 'Billing," and more. Click on the 'Skins' tab to reveal options related to changing skins. Step 4: Choose an existing skin or upload a new one Minecraft provides several default skins from which to choose if you don't already have a favorite skin downloaded. Alternatively, if you want to use a previously saved skin file, skip ahead to Step 5. To pick a preloaded skin, simply hover over any available option until its image appears fully. Then, click on it to make the selection. The selected skin will now appear as your current Minecraft character whenever you play the game online. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Make sure you preview the entire skin before selecting it since some parts might be obscured during gameplay due to environmental factors. Step 5: Upload a Custom Skin File (Optional) If you prefer using a custom skin instead of those provided by Minecraft, click on the blue button labeled 'Upload a custom skin.' Find the .png file containing your desired skin on your computer or mobile device and double-click it. Wait for the progress bar to fill completely, signifying successful upload. Afterward, refresh the page to confirm whether the upload was successful. Key Tips & Guidelines: - Save your custom skin as a .png file format for optimal results. - Check dimensions; ideal size for Minecraft skins is 64x64 pixels. - Avoid transparent backgrounds; they could cause issues with rendering. Step 6: Confirm changes Once satisfied with your choice, scroll down and hit the green 'Save Changes' button located at the bottom left side of the page. A confirmation message stating 'Your settings have been updated!' will pop up momentarily after saving the modifications. Congratulations! You have changed your Minecraft skin in less than 60 seconds. Now get ready to impress other players with your stylish avatar while exploring blocky worlds together. Once upon a time, there was a little girl who went to visit her grandma in the countryside. The girl was really excited to go to the countryside, because she has heard lots of stories about the beautiful flowers there. When she arrived, the little girl ran around and saw lots of different flowers in the garden of her grandma's house. Suddenly, the little girl stopped and saw a beautiful flower that no one else seemed to notice. It was a very small flower growing slowly in the grass. The little girl slowly reached out to pluck the flower, but her grandma suddenly said: "No sweetheart, don't pick the flower. It takes a very long time for it to grow and if you pick it, it won't be able to grow anymore." The little girl thought for a moment and then smiled. Title: How to Find the Equation of a Linear Function Using Two Given Points Linear functions are mathematical expressions that represent straight lines on a graph. They have the general form y = mx + b, where m is the slope or gradient of the line, and b is the y-intercept (the value of y when x equals zero). This article will guide you through finding the equation of a linear function using two given points. **Step 1: Identify Your Two Points** The first step in finding the equation of a linear function with two given points is actually identifying those two points. These should be coordinate pairs (x,y), written as an ordered list where the first number represents the horizontal position (x) and the second number represents the vertical position (y). For example, (2,7) means that you're at position 2 along the x-axis and position 7 along the y-axis. Key Tip: Make sure both your points are plotted correctly on the graph before proceeding. It might help to draw a quick sketch of the line defined by these points to visualize what you're doing. **Step 2: Calculate the Slope (m)** Once you have your two points, say P1(x1, y1) and P2(x2, y2), you can calculate the slope (m) using this formula: m = (y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1) This formula calculates the change in y divided by the corresponding change in x between the two points. The resulting value gives us the steepness of our line. If m is positive, the line rises from left to right; if negative, it falls from left to right. Guideline: Be careful about which point you label as P1 and which as P2. Swapping them would reverse the signs of both terms in the numerator, changing the sign of the calculated slope without affecting its absolute value. **Step 3: Determine the Y-Intercept (b)** Now that we know the slope (m), we need to find the y-intercept (b). To do so, select one of your original points, plug it into the equation y = mx + b, then solve for b. Here's how: Let's choose P1(x1, y1): Substitute known values into the equation: y1 = m*x1 + b Rearrange the equation to isolate b: b = y1 - m*x1 And there you go! You now have your y-intercept. Key Tips: Double-check your calculations here because errors made while calculating the slope may propagate into incorrect results for the y-intercept. Also remember that any valid solution for b will yield the same line since adding or subtracting constants merely shifts the line up or down without altering its orientation. **Step 4: Substitute Values into Final Form** Finally, substitute your calculated slope (m) and y-intercept (b) back into the standard equation form y = mx + b. That's it! You've found the equation of a linear function based on two given points. To verify your result, plot your equation on a graph and ensure it passes through your initial points used in steps 1 & 2. Alternatively, check whether substituting either of your original coordinates into your final equation yields the correct output. Remember that old carnival game where you had to guess how many jellybeans were in a jar? Well, guessing is more than pure fun, it's also a key part of kindergarten math. Here's how to bring it to the breakfast table! What You Need: - A box of cereal with just one general shape and size (ex. Cheerios or Chex) - A bowl and spoon - A baggie What You Do: - The word estimation sounds a bit intimidating. Start by explaining to your child that when we estimate, we're just making a good guess about how many of something we see. - Ask your child to scoop out a spoonful of cereal using the spoon. Then ask her to estimate how many pieces are on the spoon, without counting them. - Once she's made her guess, count the cereal pieces together. Ask your child, "Was your estimate close?" and "Were there more or less pieces on the spoon than you thought?" - Dump the spoonful of cereal into the plastic baggie. Then scoop a few more spoonfuls into the bag (four is plenty) and ask your child to estimate the total. Once she's guessed, count the cereal pieces in the baggie and ask the same two questions, "Was your estimate close?" and "Were there more or less pieces than you thought?" - Dump the contents of the baggie into a bowl and add some more cereal to it. Ask your child to estimate how many pieces are in the bowl. Now pour in some milk, and have her count as she eats them up, to see how close she got! The mouse and the and his daddy. The verb-subject-object (VSO) word order is the third-most common word order in world languages. There are far fewer VSO languages than SVO and SOV languages, and only 9% of them are VSO. Language groups in which VSO is common include Afroasiatic languages, such as Arabic, Hebrew and Aramaic, and Celtic languages, such as Irish, Welsh and Cornish. In VSO languages, "Robert opens the door" would be "Opens Robert the door". Spanish sentences are usually SVO, but VSO is also common. In Spanish, the example above can be as "Roberto abre la puerta" (Robert opens the door) or "Abre Roberto la puerta" (Opens Robert the door). The XXX had a flashlight out here and picked it up. Getting a medical marijuana card in Canada is a straightforward process that involves consulting with a healthcare professional and registering with a licensed producer or obtaining authorization from Health Canada. Here's a detailed guide on how to get a medical marijuana card in Canada: Step 1: Consultation with a Healthcare Professional The first step to getting a medical marijuana card in Canada is to consult with a qualified healthcare professional who can assess your condition and determine if medical cannabis is appropriate for you. This can be done through a virtual consultation or an in-person appointment. During the consultation, the healthcare professional will review your medical history, current medications, and symptoms to decide whether medical cannabis is a suitable treatment option. They may also provide recommendations on strains, dosages, and methods of consumption based on your specific needs. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Be prepared to discuss your medical conditions, symptoms, and any previous treatments thoroughly. * Bring all relevant medical records, including diagnoses, test results, and lists of current medications. * Ask questions about potential side effects, interactions with other medications, and different types of cannabis products available. * Consider seeking a second opinion if unsure about the recommendation given by the initial healthcare professional. Step 2: Register with a Licensed Producer (LP) If the healthcare professional determines that medical cannabis is appropriate for you, they will provide a written medical document outlining their recommendations. You can then register with one of Canada's licensed producers using this document. The LP will verify your registration and assign you a unique access code, which you will need when ordering medical marijuana online or over the phone. Once registered, you can place orders directly through the LP's website or customer service line. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Research various LP options before choosing one, considering factors such as product selection, pricing, shipping costs, and customer reviews. * Keep track of your registration information and order details for future reference. * Understand the return policy of the LP, in case there are issues with your order. Step 3: Ordering Medical Cannabis Once registered with an LP, you can order medical marijuana according to your healthcare professional's recommendations. Orders can typically be placed online, over the phone, or via mail. When placing an order, ensure that you have provided the correct delivery address, as well as any necessary payment information. Upon receiving your order, inspect the packaging for accuracy and quality before signing off on the delivery. Key Tips & Guidelines: * Place orders in advance to avoid running out of medication and allow time for processing and shipping. * Familiarize yourself with the LP's shipping policies and estimated delivery times. * Store your medical marijuana securely and keep it away from children and pets. Alternative Option: Authorization from Health Canada Instead of registering with an LP, some individuals may choose to obtain an authorization from Health Canada under the Access to Cannabis for Medical Purposes Regulations (ACMPR). To do so, patients must submit an application form along with supporting documentation, including a valid medical document from a healthcare professional. If approved, Health Canada will issue an authorization allowing the individual to grow their own cannabis plants or designate someone else to do so on their behalf. However, due to recent changes in regulations, growing at home is no longer permitted without prior approval from Health Canada. In summary, getting a medical marijuana card in Canada requires consulting with a healthcare professional, registering with a licensed producer, and placing orders accordingly. By following these steps and adhering to key tips and guidelines, individuals can gain legal access to medical cannabis and enjoy its potential therapeutic benefits safely and responsibly. oh, look at all these polka dots! Title: How to Create a YouTube Account: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: YouTube is one of the most popular video-sharing platforms worldwide, with billions of users and videos covering an extensive range of topics. Creating a YouTube account enables you to enjoy various benefits such as uploading your own videos, commenting on other users' content, creating playlists, and subscribing to channels that interest you. This detailed guide will walk you through every step required to establish a new YouTube account while providing helpful tips and best practices along the way. Step 1: Visit the YouTube website or open its mobile app. To start setting up your YouTube account, navigate to the platform's official site (www.youtube.com) using your preferred web browser or launch the YouTube application on your smartphone or tablet. By doing this, you can begin exploring the world of online video content right away. Step 2: Click 'Sign in' at the top-right corner of the page. Once you are on the homepage, look for the 'Sign in' button located near the upper-right section of the screen. Although signing up requires clicking 'Sign in,' don't worry - if you aren't already logged into any Google accounts, the following steps will prompt you to create a new one specifically for YouTube. Key Tip: If you want to manage multiple YouTube channels under one account later on, make sure to sign up with a new Gmail address rather than linking an existing email. Step 3: Select 'Create account.' After clicking 'Sign in', you will be redirected to a login page displaying several options like continuing with Google, Facebook, Apple ID, or phone number. Since we aim to set up a new YouTube account, click on 'More options' followed by 'Create account'. Step 4: Fill out the registration form. Now comes time to enter some personal information needed to register your new YouTube account. You need to provide details such as First name, Last name, Desired Email Address, Confirm Email Address, Password, Birthday, and Gender. Make sure all these fields are accurate since they serve essential functions during recovery processes and ensure secure access to your account. Guideline: Be cautious about sharing sensitive data when filling out forms; avoid giving false information and never share your password with anyone. Step 5: Verify your email address. Upon completing the registration form, check your email inbox for a message from Google containing a verification code. Enter this code within the provided text box on the YouTube website to confirm ownership of the given email address. Once verified, proceed by clicking 'Next'. Step 6: Personalize your profile. In this stage, you get to customize your public presence on YouTube by adding a Profile Picture and Cover Photo representing yourself or brand identity. Use high-quality images that reflect your interests or profession clearly. Additionally, write a brief yet engaging Introduction highlighting who you are and what type of content viewers should expect from your channel(s). Step 7: Agree to terms & conditions and privacy policy. Before finalizing your account creation process, take some time to read through YouTube's Terms of Service and Privacy Policy thoroughly. Familiarize yourself with their rules and regulations regarding user behavior, copyright issues, data protection, etc., then tick the boxes indicating acceptance before hitting 'Finish'. Congratulations! You now have successfully created a YouTube account. From here, explore the vast array of videos available, engage with creators by liking, sharing, and commenting on their work, and consider starting your journey as a content creator by publishing original content on your newly minted channel. Happy viewing! Title: How to Dress Punk in the Summer: A Comprehensive Guide Introduction: Punk fashion is known for its rebellious spirit, DIY aesthetic, and bold statements. However, dressing punk during summer can be challenging due to the heat and humidity. This guide will help you navigate through the process of creating a stylish yet comfortable punk-inspired outfit that's suitable for warm weather. We will cover clothing items, accessories, makeup, hairstyles, and footwear while providing helpful tips along the way. Section 1: Clothing Items Step 1: Choose Breathable Fabrics Select lightweight, breathable materials such as cotton, linen, or rayon to keep cool in hot temperatures. These fabrics allow better air circulation and absorb moisture effectively. Key Tip: Avoid synthetic materials like polyester and nylon, which trap heat and sweat against your skin. Step 2: Opt for Short Sleeves or Sleeveless Tops Go for short sleeved t-shirts, tank tops, or crop tops featuring punk-related designs like band logos, skulls, or rebellious slogans. This choice exposes more skin to promote cooling and comfort. Guideline: Make sure the fit is loose enough to prevent excessive sweating but snug enough to maintain the edgy look. Step 3: Select Summer-Friendly Bottoms Choose between shorts, skirts, or ripped jeans (if preferred) made from lightweight denim or stretchy fabric blends. A mini skirt with fishnets underneath or distressed denim shorts paired with combat boots create an iconic punk vibe. Important Note: Denim takes time to dry, so consider other options if expecting heavy perspiration. Section 2: Accessories Step 4: Don't Forget Layering Incorporate lighter layers using sheer material jackets, vests, or cardigans. They add visual interest without adding too much warmth. Step 5: Add Studded or Spiked Accessories Enhance your outfit with studded belts, chokers, bracelets, or wristbands. Leather cuffs adorned with metal spikes or safety pins are also great choices. Step 6: Rock Band Patches and Pins Show off your favorite bands by sewing patches onto backpacks, hats, or jacket lapels. Attach enamel pins to bags, collars, or hat brims for added detail. Section 3: Makeup & Hairstyles Step 7: Go Easy on Makeup Opt for minimalistic makeup looks focusing on eyeliners and lipsticks. Waterproof products ensure longevity despite the heat. Key Tips: - Use eyeshadow primers to prevent creasing. - Set your foundation with setting powder to minimize shine. This game is similar to Hangman. Draw two identical snowmen on the board, each with 10-16 parts, like two or three circles for the body, arms, buttons, eyes, nose, mouth, etc. Divide the class into two teams. A player from each team spells a winter word, defines it, reads it, or answers a question about it; whatever you are working on is fine. If the player is correct, then he erases one part of the other team's snowman. Play until one snowman has disappeared, or melted. If you start at the top, it would look more like melting, but that is not necessary. Divide into two teams. One player goes to the board and draws a picture that represents a winter word. The team that says the word first gets a point. Hint: if you have an uneven number of kids, one could be score keeper. Password is a good word game. Here is a good version of it for the classroom that you could call "Clue" or "What is That?" Pick two students to stand at the front of the room facing the class. Write the word on the board or show it to the rest of the class on a piece of paper. The students raise their hand if they have a good clue and the two players take turns calling on one of them. Either of the two can guess the answers. Whoever guesses correctly remains and the student who gave the clue takes the other player's spot. It doesn't matter if it doesn't match, huh? In our Observing Worms activity, you will see how worms are a necessary part of nature's delicate balance. What You'll Need: Strong magnifying glass For this activity, you'll need to find some good-sized worms to observe. You can dig in soft, moist garden soil to find lots of small ones. To find big nightcrawlers, go out onto a healthy lawn at night with a flashlight. You might try lifting a large stone or flowerpot, or digging under the ground's surface. Once you've got your worm, wet your fingers and run them down both sides of the worm at once. You may be surprised to feel short, stiff bristles (called "setae") that help the worm move through the soil. Put the worm on a dry piece of paper and let it crawl. You will hear a faint scratching noise as the setae rub on the paper. Notice how the worm moves. Strong muscles running up and down the body shorten it, while ring-shaped muscles squeeze it to make it longer. Write down your findings and make drawings of your worm. Always put the worms back where you found them when you are done. After observing individual worms, watch worms making tracks in our next activity, wiggly workers, on the following page. Title: How to Use Dental Floss to Prevent Cavities Dental floss is an effective tool in preventing cavities by removing plaque and food particles from areas where your toothbrush can't reach. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the process of using dental floss correctly and provide additional tips to maximize its benefits. **Step 1: Choose the Right Type of Dental Floss** There are two main types of dental floss: nylon (multifilament) and PTFE (monofilament). Nylon floss comes in various flavors and thicknesses, while PTFE floss slides more smoothly between teeth but may be more expensive. Consider any dental concerns, such as tight spaces or bridgework, when selecting the right type for you. *Key tip*: Look for ADA-accepted products to ensure quality and safety. **Step 2: Break Off About 18 Inches of Floss** This length allows enough slack to grip the floss with both hands and maneuver it around each tooth without reusing the same section. *Guideline*: Wind most of the floss around the middle finger of one hand, leaving about 2 inches free. Then wrap the remaining floss around the middle finger of your other hand, this will serve as your clean floss reserve. **Step 3: Hold the Floss Tautly Between Thumbs and Index Fingers** Gently slide the floss between your teeth using a back-and-forth motion. Be careful not to snap the floss into gum tissue, which could cause injury. *Key tip*: Curve the floss into a C shape against the side of each tooth to ensure maximum contact with tooth surfaces. **Step 4: Move the Floss Up and Down Along the Tooth Surface** Once the floss reaches the gum line, gently move it up and down to scrape off plaque. Repeat this action for every tooth surface, including behind rear molars. *Guideline*: Remember to use fresh sections of floss as you progress, unwinding new segments from the finger with more wrapped floss. **Step 5: Rinse Your Mouth After Flossing** Rinsing removes loosened debris and leaves your mouth feeling fresher. It also helps prevent swallowed floss, although incidental swallowing is generally harmless. *Key tip*: Swish vigorously with water or mouthwash for at least 30 seconds before spitting out. **Additional Tips:** - **Floss Daily**: Ideally, floss once per day, preferably before bedtime to remove plaque accumulated throughout the day. - **Be Patient**: It might take several attempts to master proper flossing technique; don't get discouraged if you struggle initially. - **Consider Interdental Brushes**: For those with braces or larger gaps between teeth, interdental brushes may be easier to use than traditional floss. - **Regular Dental Checkups**: Regular professional cleaning and examination by a dentist are crucial for maintaining oral health and catching potential issues early. By incorporating these steps and guidelines into your daily routine, you'll significantly reduce your risk of developing cavities and improve overall oral hygiene. Clear Filters in Excel: A Comprehensive Tutorial =============================================== Microsoft Excel is a powerful tool used for data analysis and manipulation. One common feature that users take advantage of is filtering. Filtering allows you to view specific subsets of your data by temporarily hiding rows based on selected criteria. However, sometimes you may want to clear these filters to display all records again or apply new filters. This tutorial will guide you through clearing filters in Microsoft Excel using various methods. Methods Covered --------------- 1. Clearing Filters Using the 'Data' Ribbon Tab 2. Clearing Filters Manually 3. Automatically Clearing Filters with VBA Code 4. Title: How to Properly Dispose of Knives Introduction: sharpening or breaking your knife, there will come a time when you need to dispose of it. However, throwing knives in the trash or recycling bin can be dangerous and may even damage machinery at recycling facilities. To safely get rid of your old or damaged knives, follow these steps carefully. Key Tips and Guidelines: 1. Check local regulations: Before disposing of any sharp objects like knives, check with your local waste management facility or government website for specific regulations regarding knife disposal. Some areas have special collection events or designated drop-off locations for hazardous household waste, including knives. 2. Choose an appropriate container: Select a sturdy box or container that can securely hold the knife without risk of injury during transport. A small cardboard box, plastic storage container, or metal tin would work well. Avoid using flimsy materials that could accidentally cut through. 3. Pad the container: Place bubble wrap, newspaper, or other cushioning material inside the chosen container to further protect yourself and others from accidental cuts while handling the discarded knife. This also prevents the blade from becoming damaged during transportation. 4. Secure the knife within the container: Using tape, firmly attach the knife (handle first) to the bottom of the container so that the blade is completely covered by padding. Make sure the tape does not cover the handle, as this could make it difficult for someone sorting through the garbage to identify the object as a knife. 5. Label the container: Clearly mark the outside of the container with a warning label indicating that it contains a sharp object. You can create your own label or print one online. Examples include "Caution: Sharp Objects" or "Danger - Contains Knife." 6. Dispose of the container properly: Once the knife has been secured and labeled, place the container in your regular garbage bag or bin for pickup. Do not put it in your recycling bin, as it poses a safety hazard to waste management workers and recycling equipment. 7. Alternative options: If you'd prefer not to throw away your knife, consider repurposing it into another useful tool or piece of home decor. For example, dull kitchen knives can be turned into garden trowels or hooks for hanging pots and pans. Get creative and give new life to your old blades! 8. Donate or sell functional knives: If your knife is still in good condition but no longer needed, consider donating it to a local charity shop or selling it online. Someone else might find it useful and appreciate having access to an affordable knife. 9. Safely store unused knives: Keep extra knives stored in a safe location where they won't pose a danger to family members or pets. Use a dedicated knife block, magnetic strip, or locked cabinet to prevent accidents. Conclusion: Properly disposing of knives ensures the safety of everyone involved in the waste removal process and reduces the likelihood of injuries caused by improperly discarded sharp objects. By following these simple steps, you can help protect your community and its resources. yeah, it seemed like especially the bee. Do you know what Carter was before? Each hand shows the time it is named for and moves around the face of the clock one complete rotation for each movement to the next larger hand. For example, the second hand moves around the face of the clock in 60 seconds. It moves once each second. When it moves all the way around the clock, the minute hand moves forward one space. When the minute hand moves all the way around the face of the clock (which takes 60 minutes), the hour hand moves forward one section. The second and minute hands take 60 movements to move all around the face of the clock, the hour hand only needs 12 movements to do the same. The 2-dimensional (2D) shape (like a circle, square, triangle, etc.) that a cube is made of is squares. The sides (faces) of a cube are squares. The edges are straight lines. The corners (vertices) are at right angles. A cube has 8 corners, 12 edges and 6 faces, as in the most usual kind of dice. A tesseract carries this idea into the fourth dimension (4D) and is made of 8 cubes. Title: Emily the Excellent Elephant When children are beginning to read and write, vowels are very important concepts that need to be covered. Short vowels, and long vowels, are the building blocks of our language. By being phonemically aware, students will be able to recognize vowels and their sounds with ease and be able to successfully blend whole words together. This lesson will guide students to understand the correspondence of e = /e/ and help students in reading and writing with this correspondence. 1. Copy of Red Gets Fed 2. Large-print typed copy of the tongue twister "Emily the excellent elephant excites the crowd with her energy" Phoneme graphic of a creaky door (door that looks old) 4. List of words for students to compare sounds (Fred/Press, fed/meat, green/bed, pen/marker) 6. Whiteboard markers 7. Letterboxes for each student 8. Letter manipulatives for each student (l,e,d,n,b,l,l,s,t,m,p) 9. List of words for letterbox lesson (led,end,bed,bell,set,smell,sled,best,send,spent) 10. Paper and pen for running records 1. I will begin the lesson by explaining that without language, it would be very difficult for us to communicate our wants and needs and ideas to one another. I will then explain that the grapheme we will be covering is e = /e/. Next, I'll model the word for the students. "The sound 'eeeh' can be found in words like Fred and press. Can you say Fred? "Now can you say press? Great job!!" 2. Next I will explain that the short e sound is like an old creaky door "eeeeh". I will show the class the phoneme graphic of the old door. "Okay, now I want everybody to make the old creaky door sound with me, ready?" [everyone] "eeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeehhhhh?" "Great job!" 3. We will then go over our poster with the tongue twister on it. "Okay, I want everybody to listen carefully to the tongue twister because not all of the eeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeh sounds come at the beginning. "Emily the excellent elephant excites the crowd with her enegy" Eeeemily theee eeexceeeeleeent eeeeleeephant eeexcites theeee crowd with her eeeenergy" "Eeeeeeeeeexcelleeeeeeeeent! Now let's all do it together again!" The whole class will then say the tongue twister together, extending the eeeeeh sounds when they come to them. 4. I will then ask students to compare sets of words with each other. "Now we're going to look at word pairs. I'm going to read two words out and you'll tell me whether you hear eeeeeh in one word or the other. Are you ready? Do you hear eeeh in fed or meat? Do you hear eeeh in green or bed? Do you hear eeeh in pen or marker? Good job! You did eeeeeeeexcellent!" Next we will do a letterbox lesson to spell words. I will hand student a set of boxes to use for the lesson. I will then the letterbox lesson, I am going to read out a word, and I want you to in your boxes. Now, don't forget that each little box represents sound in the word. For example [I will turn and draw three boxes board to portray my letterboxes] if I were to spell the word peck, I listen for the sound eeeeeh and place it in its letterbox. So I will put an e in the box. Then I will focus on the consonants and place the p in its letterbox. Finally, I'll go to the last sound of the word But I know that it is ck because it is a digraph that follows a short since c-k makes one sound, both of those letters will go in the last means I will have 3 letterboxes for the word peck. 6. After I demonstrate the letterbox lesson, I will begin to administer the lesson to the class by reading off the following words as they spell them: 3: [led, end, bed, bell, set, bad, dad]; 4: [smell, sled, best, send, small]; 5: [spent]. 7. After we finish the spelling portion of the lesson, I will write the words on the white board and have the students read them to me one by one. The students will make sure and identify the eeeeh in each word. This will show me if they understand the eeeeh sound in spoken and written words. 8. "Everyone has done a fabulous job with our lesson today! Now I am going to write some pretend words on the board for everyone to read. These words are not real but they can still be read." I will then write the following pseudo words on the board : leb, sem, nep, pem. This will be my assessment. 9. "Great job with the letterbox lesson! We're going to read the book Red Gets Fed. This book is about a dog named Red who is really hungry in the morning and needs to find somebody to feed him. Do you think Red gets fed? Do any of you have a dog at home? Well, let us read our story to find out!" I will assess the students' reading of Red Gets Fed by taking a running record while they read if time permits. The students will have already read the book Red Gets Fed earlier this week since we only take running records on books students have already read with no scaffolding. If time does not permit write new pseudowords on the white board that focus on e = /e/ and get students to call them out. Cushman, Sheila. Red Gets Fed. Murray, Bruce. The White, Amy. Some fruits that are called "berries" in English are not "true berries" by the use of words above. These include raspberries, strawberry, blackberries, cranberries, and boysenberries. Title: How to Find Out the Gender of a Kitten the Easy Way Introduction: Determining the gender of a kitten can be both exciting and confusing, especially for first-time cat owners or those new to handling young cats. This guide will walk you through the simple process using a method that is non-invasive, easy, and reliable. By following these steps, you'll be able to accurately determine your kitten's gender with confidence. **Key Tips:** 1. Perform this examination in a quiet space where the kitten feels safe and secure. 2. Have patience as some kittens might be wriggly and difficult to handle at first. 3. If possible, have someone assist you during this process, making it easier to hold the kitten steady. 4. Always wash your hands before and after handling any animal to maintain hygiene. 5. Remember that even experienced individuals may sometimes make mistakes, so double-check if unsure. **Step 1: Familiarize Yourself With Cat Anatomy** To correctly identify the gender of a kitten, understanding its anatomical differences is crucial. The primary distinction lies between their reproductive organs - male kittens possess testicles while female ones have ovaries and uteruses. However, since externalizing these parts for observation would be impractical and invasive, we rely instead on identifying secondary sexual characteristics - specifically, the distance between the anus and urethral opening (also known as the genital papilla). **Step 2: Holding the Kitten Correctly** Place the kitten gently but firmly on its back in your lap or on a flat surface. Position one hand under its belly and chest, providing support and preventing escape attempts. Use your thumb and index finger of the other hand to spread apart the fur around the anal region. Be gentle; too much pressure could cause discomfort or injury. ![Holding a kitten]( Let's get some other farm animals and put them on the train. Terrorism has no official criminal law definition at the international level. Common definitions of terrorism refer to the violent acts which are intended to create fear (terror). Kindergarten Lesson Plans Autumn theme - teach math and science using pumpkins... Teach hands on kindergarten lesson plans by using pumpkins. Pumpkins are tough enough to withstand lots of hands on activities. Purchase a large pumpkin and a few smaller ones in various sizes to provide your students with science and math experiences. Put the pumpkins in the science center for a few days before using them for teaching. At center times let the children take turns holding the pumpkins to compare how heavy they are and to feel the texture of the pumpkin shells. Include pumpkin stories, cooking pumpkin muffins and pumpkin Geometry Jacks in your kindergarten lesson plans to keep your students engaged. Kindergarten lesson plan 1 - Measuring the pumpkin's height... Gather string and unifix blocks. 1. Have the children sit on the carpet and tell them that you are going to measure how tall the large pumpkin is from the floor to the top of the stem by lining up unifix blocks. 2. Count the blocks as a group. 3. Record the answer on a chart like the one below. Kindergarten lesson plan 2 - Measuring the pumpkin's circumference... 1. Ask the students if they can think of a way to measure how many unifix blocks it would take to go around the middle of the pumpkin (show them the middle). Introduce the word, "circumference". 2. After the children have had a chance to talk and think about it, wrap a string around the pumpkin and then measure how long the string is with the blocks. 3. Have the group of children count the blocks and then record the answer on the chart. Save the bottom part of the chart to record the number of seeds in the pumpkin. Follow up: Pumpkin width graph lesson Materials: Give each student... - one 8 inch orange paper - brown rectangles about 1 inch x 2 inches - unifix blocks (one inch blocks that hook together) - make a graph on a piece of chart paper similar to the one below for students to tape their pumpkins on 1. Students cut a pumpkin shape any size they want out of their piece of orange paper then glue the brown stem on their pumpkin. 2. Students lay unifix blocks across their pumpkins and count how many blocks wide their pumpkin is. 3. Students print their number on their pumpkin 4. Students tape their pumpkin above the correct number of blocks on the graph. 5. Kindergarten lesson plan 3 - Observe the pumpkin inside and out... 1. Have the children touch, smell, and look at the outside of the pumpkin. 2. Record their words on a chart similar to the one on the left. 3. Tell the students there are seeds in the pumpkin and have them estimate how many seeds are in the pumpkin. 4. Print their guesses on the first chart. 5. Cut the pumpkin in half and repeat the process. 6. Record the children's comments. 7. During center time let the children take turns picking the seeds out of the pumpkin and putting them on cookie sheets to dry. Provide a bowl for the pulp. Some children will not want to do this and others will love it. Kindergarten lesson plan - Count the seeds Materials: Dried pumpkin seeds, small squares of paper. 1. After the seeds have dried for a day or two, pile them on the tables and ask children to count groups of five seeds (or ten if your students are able) and place them on a square of paper. 2. When all the seeds are counted out on squares, as a group count by 5s to 100 and group those seeds. 3. Count another group of 100 etc. until all the seeds are counted. 4. Record the approximate number of seeds on the chart next to the children's estimates. All pumpkin pages: 1. Kindergarten Lesson Plans pumpkin theme - teach science and math with pumpkins! 2.
